3.name: Venusaur
3.overview: Venusaur finds a niche on sun teams as a fast wallbreaker in NU. In sun, Venusaur can force progress against common defensive Pokemon such as Vaporeon, Wo-Chien, and Bellibolt, using them as setup fodder with Growth. Its Grass / Poison typing also gives it setup opportunities against Pokemon like Paldean Tauros-W and…

sets.name: Sun Sweeper
sets.description: Venusaur uses Chlorophyll and Growth to become a deadly sun sweeper, capable of outspeeding even boosted foes like +1 Iron Valiant and Iron Moth. Venusaur is capable of sustaining itself long-term with Giga Drain, while Weather Ball vaporizes Steel-types like Gholdengo and Corviknight and Grass-types like Meowscarada…

sets.name: Offensive
sets.description:
Life Orb makes Venusaur harder to switch around, guaranteeing that it can 2HKO foes like Alolan Sandslash and, after Stealth Rock, Assault Vest Galarian Slowbro. That said, this power boost isn't often necessary, and Heavy-Duty Boots is usually better for improving Venusaur's longevity.
Sample set. Stay tuned for…

sets.name: Sun Sweeper
sets.description:
Charizard is one of the strongest sweepers in the metagame thanks to the sun, Solar Power, and Choice Specs. With these damage multipliers, Charizard is capable of OHKOing and 2HKOing the entire metagame with its Fire-type attacks.
Heat Wave is Charizard's most used attack, providing good spread damage. Weather Ball…

sets.name: Sun Attacker
sets.description:
An alternative EV spread of 192 HP / 28 SpA / 36 SpD / 252 Spe (Modest Nature) survives Thunderclap from Raging Bolt. Another alternative EV spread of 148 HP / 84 Def / 24 SpA / 252 Spe (Modest Nature) survives Double-Edge from Mega Salamence. Both EV spreads outspeed Glimmora.
Scorching Sands should be used on teams…

sets.name: Drought Wallbreaker
sets.description: Mega Charizard Y's immense power combined with Drought makes it an extremely dangerous wallbreaker that threatens common defensive staples like Alomomola, Melmetal, and Ferrothorn while also supporting its teammates via sun. Weather Ball is the preferred Fire-type STAB move, as it is perfectly accurate, quite…

sets.name: Please Don't Use Mega Beedrill
sets.description: Despite Mega Beedrill initially seeming like a good Pokemon thanks to its exceptional Attack and Speed stats, great ability in Adaptability, and access to U-turn, it is completely unviable. While U-turn can grab momentum against foes such as Hydreigon and Mega Latias, and Poison Jab has some utility in targeting…

sets.name: Bird Jesus (Normal)
sets.description: Mega Pidgeot is one of the premier special attackers Normal teams have access to because of its good Speed tier and a powerful, coveted STAB move in Hurricane that beats nuisances to Normal teams like Kommo-o and Ogerpon-W while never missing. Heat Wave complements Hurricane well by heavily damaging Steel-types like…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description: Ekans is an alternative Fighting-resistant Pokemon that can make progress through its utility moves and Intimidate. Knock Off allows for guaranteed progress against Pokemon like Mienfoo, Mudbray, and Foongus. Earthquake coverage hits Electric-types, such as Elekid and Chinchou, as well as Tinkatink and Poison-types…

sets.name: Surge Surfer (Electric)
sets.description: Alolan Raichu surfs on Electric Terrain, blitzing past even the fastest Choice Scarf users in Greninja and Meowscarada and Swift Swim and Sand Rush users in Mega Swampert and Excadrill, which threaten to overwhelm Electric teams. Nasty Plot provides Alolan Raichu with the much-needed boost to OHKO Gliscor, Sinistcha,…

sets.name: Sweeper
sets.description: Alolan Sandshrew carves out a neat role in LC as both a sweeper and utility Pokemon. Its Ice / Steel typing makes it one of the few reliable Flying-resistant Pokemon in the tier. Triple Axel gives Alolan Sandshrew strong STAB coverage, and Earthquake hits the Fire- and Steel-types that Triple Axel cannot. Building off…

28.name: Sandslash
28.overview: Sandslash is one of the few good entry hazard removers in ZU, and it's distinguished as a role compressor due to its additional access to Stealth Rock and Spikes. Spinblockers like Froslass and Trevenant fear and despise Sandslash's Knock Off, and Sandslash matches up nicely against common hazard setters like Regirock…

sets.name: Specially Defensive Spinner
sets.description: Alolan Sandslash is a great role compressor, as it can remove entry hazards and set up its own, and on top of this, it can also check threats like Meloetta, Florges, and Rotom-C. While Triple Axel is more powerful than Ice Spinner, it must land all three times to do so, making the latter a more accurate alternative.…

sets.name: Rapid Spin
sets.description: Good physical bulk, solid Attack, and plenty of utility make Sandslash an excellent hazard remover and role compressor. Knock Off deters spinblockers like Trevenant, nails Mesprit, and punishes Ground-immune Pokemon like Vikavolt, Charizard, and Orthworm. Knock Off's item removal helps soften up Hattrem and renders…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description: Sandslash is potent wallbreaker thanks to its decent bulk and good defensive typing that create opportunities for it to set up with Swords Dance and open holes in teams with its great offensive coverage and blistering Speed under sand. Knock Off is Sandslash's best blanket coverage move for complementing Earthquake,…

sets.name: Wallbreaker (Poison)
sets.description: Nidoking finds a solid place on Poison teams as the premier special attacker thanks to Sheer Force and its solid movepool giving it a unique role of helping with the Steel matchup. Earth Power demolishes the Steel- and Poison-types that can easily come in on its teammates such as Heatran, Gholdengo, and Toxapex.…

sets.name: Redirection Support
sets.description:
Set Details
Clefairy is a great support tool for partners like setup Calyrex-S due to its ability Friend Guard and access to Follow Me to redirect attacks.
After You is a good tool in Trick Room thanks to Clefairy's low Speed, allowing Calyrex-S to move before threats such as Calyrex-I, Iron Hands, and Ursaluna.…

sets.name: Bulky Support
sets.description: Clefairy is an excellent support Pokemon that utilizes its combination of Friend Guard and Follow Me to support frail partners. Sing is used to disable foes, while After You helps fast teams by letting its allies move before common checks like Calyrex-I in Trick Room. Clefairy can also opt for Heal Pulse to keep its…

sets.name: Redirection Support
sets.description: Clefable is able to easily and safely redirect attacks from opposing Pokemon with Follow Me to keep its allies safe, and with Unaware, it redirects even boosted attacks without worry. Clefable, with its Fairy typing, does well into the Dragon-infested format of Regulation H, threatening Pokemon such as Baxcalibur,…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description: Leveraging its passable bulk, amazing typing, solid movepool, a fantastic ability in Magic Guard, Clefable establishes its niche in the OU tier as a solid glue on bulkier team compositions, which appreciate its amazing role compression. Clefable maximizes its physical bulk to handle a myriad of physical attackers and…

sets.name: Calm Mind
sets.description: Clefable can function as a great sweeper, as it uses its good bulk, fantastic typing, and useful ability to find itself opportunities to set up. Moonblast can potentially drop the Special Attack of opposing Pokemon like Primarina, making Clefable harder to break. Moonlight is required to give Clefable a healing…

sets.name: Unaware
sets.description: Unaware Clefable is an excellent stopgap to many dangerous setup sweepers, such as Zamazenta and Iron Valiant, halting them in their tracks and sometimes even acting as a wincon in its own right. While other Unaware Pokemon can potentially fill these needs, Clefable stands out with its solid Fairy typing, diverse…

38.name: Ninetales-Alola
38.overview:
Weather control in Snow Warning and Aurora Veil support give Alolan Ninetales a strong niche in the metagame. Alolan Ninetales's great offensive typing allows it to threaten Chien-Pao, Urshifu-S, and Landorus with an OHKO while also walling the former two.
Alolan Ninetales pairs especially well with slower Ice-types…

sets.name: Offensive
sets.description:
Offensive Alolan Ninetales is a good Pokemon in Regulation H thanks to its fast STAB Blizzard in snow, threatening metagame staples like Rillaboom, Dragonite, and the Ursaluna formes, and its ability to support its team in a pinch thanks to its wide supportive movepool.
Freeze-Dry grants Alolan Ninetales the ability…

sets.name: Screens Setter
sets.description:
Alolan Ninetales also finds a good niche as a supportive screens setter thanks to its good Speed tier and access to a variety of other support tools.
This Alolan Ninetales focuses more on setting up Aurora Veil and disrupting with Encore so its team can set up and sweep more comfortably rather than threatening bulky…

sets.name: Screen Setter
sets.description:
Moonblast guarantees a 2HKO on Urshifu-R. Helping Hand helps make up for Alolan Ninetales's poor damage output.
Light Clay is used to make sure Aurora Veil lasts for the majority of the game.
The defensive investment allows Alolan Ninetales to survive Choice Specs Flutter Mane's Moonblast and avoid the 2HKO from…

sets.name: Snow Setter
sets.description:
Alolan Ninetales is the premier snow setter due to its Speed tier and utility. It enables offensive structures that flourish with Aurora Veil support, reducing damage from opposing Pokemon.
Alolan Ninetales runs Freeze-Dry in order to force super effective damage on prominent Water-types like Ogerpon-W.
Icy Wind is a…

50.name: Diglett-Alola
50.overview: Alolan Diglett is an excellent wallbreaker and late-game cleaner in Little Cup, with a blazing Speed tier and dangerous STAB combination. With solid coverage and the power boost from Life Orb, Alolan Diglett can OHKO or 2HKO most of the tier. Despite its strengths, Alolan Diglett is very frail, making it difficult to…

sets.name: Life Orb
sets.description: Earthquake threatens OHKOs on Pokemon such as Glimmet, Tinkatink, and Chinchou. Rock Blast threatens the Flying-types that otherwise would give Alolan Diglett trouble, notably Vullaby due to the interaction with Weak Armor. Sucker Punch helps Alolan Diglett pick off weakened foes that Speed tie or outspeed it, such as…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Mankey is one of the most powerful Choice Scarf users in Little Cup thanks to its high Speed, which allows it to outpace or tie with the entire unboosted metagame. This allows Mankey to beat other common Choice Scarf users such as Magnemite, Mudbray, and Gothita. U-turn allows it to greatly capitalize on the switches…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Hisuian Growlithe serves as a potent wallbreaker with a Choice Scarf. Its ability to spam high-Base Power moves makes it a difficult Pokemon to switch into, often forcing guessing games between Flare Blitz and Head Smash. Psychic Fangs deters more reliable switch-ins to Hisuian Growlithe's STAB moves, hitting Mienfoo…

sets.name: Eviolite
sets.description: Eviolite Hisuian Growlithe is an amazing wallbreaker that trades the immediate speed of Choice Scarf sets for more utility options and the ability to switch moves, boasting powerful attacks that can 2HKO almost any foe without the drawback of recoil. Stealth Rock helps rack up passive damage on opposing Pokemon, while…

59.name: Arcanine-Hisui
59.overview:
Hisuian Arcanine is a potent offensive Pokemon on many teams thanks to its good Attack, great STAB coverage, and excellent ability in Intimidate, giving it good matchups against Tornadus, Rillaboom, and Chien-Pao.
Hisuian Arcanine works best in Tailwind compositions where its average Speed and poor defensive typing…

sets.name: Clear Amulet
sets.description:
With great Attack and decent Speed, good utility in Intimidate, and a good offensive typing in Fire/Rock, Hisuian Arcanine can be a solid offensive pick for teams, threatening metagame staples such as Rillaboom, Gholdengo, Incineroar, and Dragonite.
Flare Blitz is Hisuian Arcanine's best STAB attack, threatening…

sets.name: Mirror Herb
sets.description:
Mirror Herb lets Hisuian Arcanine take advantage of Attack boosts from Swords Dance Kingambit, Swords Dance Sneasler, and Bulk Up Annihilape and remove them with boosted Flare Blitz. It can gain Speed boosts from other setup sweepers like Quiver Dance Volcarona and Scale Shot Dragonite.
Close Combat threatens the…

sets.name: Choice Band
sets.description:
Tera Blast Fairy can hit foes like Urshifu-R, Urshifu, and Raging Bolt, the former of which walls Hisuian Arcanine's STABs. Head Smash can be used to threaten Pokemon like Incineroar and Ogerpon-W with an OHKO at the cost of being chipped down by recoil.
Choice Band boosts Hisuian Arcanine's damage, allowing for OHKOs…

sets.name: Bulk Up
sets.description: 96 Spe allows Poliwrath to outspeeds uninvested Milotic, Jolly Rhydon, and 252 Spe Hariyama.

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description: Alakazam is a powerful offensive threat thanks to Nasty Plot, its high Speed, and its ability allowing it to ignore entry hazards and other forms of passive damage to keep its Focus Sash intact. These assets make it a great revenge killer or wallbreaker for offensive teams. It is able to outspeed and KO top-tier…

sets.name: Focus Sash
sets.description: This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

73.name: Tentacruel
73.overview:
Poison
Tentacruel is a surprising option on Poison teams, as Toxapex's incredible bulk and longevity completely outclass it as a defensive wall in a majority of situations. However, Tentacruel has enough unique tools such that it could be worth considering over Toxapex should a more proactive Water-type threat be…

sets.name: Defensive Pivot
sets.description: Tentacruel is outclassed by Brambleghast, Scyther, and Altaria as entry hazard removal that double up as a Fighting-type check.

sets.name: Real Squid [Rapid Spin] (Poison)
sets.description: Tentacruel finds a niche on Poison teams thanks to a flurry of unique traits over its competition in Toxapex such as a vastly superior Speed stat and tools such as Rapid Spin, which makes Tentacruel the only form of entry hazard removal on Poison teams not weak to Steel-type attacks, allowing it to comfortably spin…

sets.name: Rapid Spin (Water)
sets.description: Tentacruel is one of Water's best forms of role compression, removing entry hazards thanks to its access to Rapid Spin, absorbing Toxic Spikes placed by Toxapex and indirectly by Glimmora with its Poison typing, and slow pivoting with Flip Turn. Aside from this, its great ability in Liquid Ooze allows it to punish the…

sets.name: Choice Band (Rock)
sets.description:
Choice Band lets Alolan Golem OHKO Bisharp with Brick Break.
This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

80.name: Slowbro
80.overview: Thanks to its typing, high HP and Defense, and access to Regenerator, Slowbro does a fantastic job at slowing down or outright stopping some of the most dangerous wallbreakers and setup sweepers of the tier in their tracks, including the likes of Entei, Krookodile, Terrakion, and Barraskewda. In addition to being one…

sets.name: Calm Mind
sets.description: Calm Mind Slowbro makes for one of the best physical walls and late-game cleaners in the metagame. It acts as an excellent check to a wide variety of Pokemon such as Terrakion, Barraskewda, and Jirachi. Calm Mind allows Slowbro to turn into a threatening late-game cleaner capable of ending games easily. Scald can…

sets.name: Future Sight
sets.description: Slowbro is among the best physically defensive walls in the tier with great bulk, multiple forms of recovery, and the ability to use Terastallization very efficiently against a good portion of the metagame. Scald allows Slowbro to punish switch-ins to Dark-types that come in to stop Future Sight, such as Bisharp and…

sets.name: Calm Mind
sets.description:
Set Details
Galarian Slowbro is a competent physical wall that can fulfill multiple roles, such as spreading poison, forming Regenerator cores, and being a bulky setup sweeper. In conjunction with its exceptional physical bulk, Calm Mind can make it a herculean task to bring down Galarian Slowbro after a few boosts,…

82.name: Magneton
82.overview: Magneton secures itself a place in the ZU metagame by virtue of its excellent power, a typing that is useful both offensively and defensively, and its ability to reliably pivot against many teams. Magneton's Steel typing is very rare in ZU, and it can hold Eviolite to shore up its mediocre bulk. This gives it a unique…

sets.name: Choice Specs
sets.description: Choice Specs Magneton is one of the most feared wallbreakers in the entire tier. Many teams are completely incapable of safely switching into Magneton thanks to its great STAB combination and access to Analytic, which amps up its power even further. Volt Switch complements Magneton nicely, as it is still quite…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Choice Scarf turns Magneton into an effective revenge killer and general anti-offense Pokemon, as it outruns the entire unboosted metagame bar Jolteon. It also has a limited, but still notable, defensive profile, and its ability to pivot brings a lot of utility to the table. Volt Switch allows Magneton to keep up…

sets.name: Wallbreaker
sets.description: Thanks to a solid typing with one weakness and good defensive stats, Alolan Grimer is able to use Knock Offs consistently throughout a game, weakening the opposing team for its teammates to take advantage of. Gunk Shot is used to hit Pokemon that have already been hit by Knock Off, while Drain Punch OHKOs Pawniard and…

89.name: Muk-Alola
89.overview: Utilizing its large special bulk and the incredible progress-making combination of Poison Touch and Knock Off, Alolan Muk stands out as a potent special wall in the tier. Common special attackers such as Gengar and Yanmega struggle to get past it and are threatened by Knock Off. Alolan Muk can be incredibly difficult…

sets.name: Ghostbuster (Stall)
sets.description:
Alolan Muk is one of the strongest dedicated checks to Calyrex-S for stall teams, boasting incredible Special Defense and a dual typing that isn't weak to Calyrex-S's most common Tera types or Psyshock.
Poison Jab: Combining Poison Jab with Poison Touch gives an amazing 51% chance to inflict poison per hit, making…

sets.name: Specially Defensive
sets.description: Possessing a unique typing and decent bulk, Alolan Muk is one of the best Calyrex-S checks on stall teams, particularly against Tera Fairy sets. Thanks to this typing and Fairy neutrality, Alolan Muk comfortably takes on Calyrex-S's STAB moves, along with checking Tera Fairy Calyrex-S better than other Dark-types like…

sets.name: RestTalk
sets.description:
Set Details
Drain Punch allows Alolan Muk to regain health from targets while helping it against Steel-types like Hisuian Goodra and Bisharp. Haze allows Alolan Muk to stop setup sweepers such as Calm Mind Slowbro, Suicune, and Reuniclus by negating the boosts they have attained. Rest is Alolan Muk's main form of…

91.name: Cloyster
91.overview:
Water
Cloyster is generally outclassed on Water teams, its poor defensive typing and lacking offensive prowess before setting up compared to options like Primarina and Urshifu-R makes finding that turn to get in and use Shell Smash difficult. However, should it overcome these hurdles, Cloyster can be a meance being…

sets.name: Shell Smash Sweeper
sets.description:
Shell Smash and Skill Link more than make up for Cloyster's weak Attack stat, making it a formidable sweeper that Focus Sash and Mimikyu can't easily stop.
Ice Shard: Priority may seem like overkill, but it helps pick off weakened threats without setup. It also gets around priority attacks from Breloom, Dragapult, and…

sets.name: Shell Smash (Water)
sets.description: Thanks to its excellent offensive movepool and access to Shell Smash, Cloyster finds a niche on Water teams. Thanks to Cloyster's high Defense stat, it can reliably use Shell Smash in front of some of the tier's premier physical attackers, such as Ceruledge and Mamoswine. Ice Beam is a great option to hit physically…

sets.name: Shell Smash Sweeper (Ice)
sets.description: Cloyster is one of Ice's best setup sweepers thanks to its access to Shell Smash in addition to its high Defense stat, which gives it opportunities to set up on the tier's physical attackers like Garchomp, Iron Treads, and Ceruledge. Icicle Spear targets opposing Flying-, Ground-, and Dragon-type Pokemon such as…

94.name: Gengar
94.overview: With Paradox Pokemon being introduced, Series 2 is bringing big changes to Battle Stadium Singles! This is a modified Series 1 analysis that takes Paradox Pokemon into account. We will update this overview and analysis again once the metagame settles more. For more information on Series 2, check out the resource…

sets.name: Disruptor
sets.description:
Gengar's useful immunities and great support movepool give it a niche as an incredibly disruptive Pokemon, especially if taking the correct predictive risks.
Destiny Bond: Destiny Bond is excellent at making foes second-guess attacking Gengar. When slower foes don't take the bait and use a setup move, Gengar can…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description:
Set Details
Nasty Plot Gengar is a potentially devastating setup sweeper thanks to its great Speed tier, outpacing base 100 Speed Pokemon like Jirachi and Entei, and strong STAB combination. Focus Blast gives Gengar the coverage to hit bulky Steel-types like Bisharp, Hisuian Goodra, and Registeel and Gengar's primary…

sets.name: Hex
sets.description:
Set Details
Hex takes advantage of status spread by Gengar and its teammates, notably Thunder Wave from users like Reuniclus and Slowbro, to bolster its damage to seemingly unwallable range. Sludge Bomb is used over Sludge Wave because of the increased odds to poison, which can similarly help to enable Hex damage.…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description:
Set Details
Choice Scarf Gengar is one of the fastest Pokemon in RU, leaving speed demons like Cyclizar and Barraskewda as well as Choice Scarf users like Krookodile and Gardevoir in the dust. Ghost / Poison is an excellent offensive typing that can hit most of the metagame for at least neutral damage, only resisted…

sets.name: Pivot
sets.description: Hisuian Electrode is an effective pivot in PU thanks to its incredible Speed stat that lets it outspeed the entire unboosted tier, great STAB combination, and useful utility options. It is so fast that it is not reliant on Choice Scarf, allowing Hisuian Electrode to find a niche over its most direct competitor,…

sets.name: Tank
sets.description: Koffing establishes itself as an excellent physical tank in Little Cup due to its high base Defense stat and Poison typing, which let it check physical attackers like Mienfoo and Timburr. Koffing's best use comes from its ability Neutralizing Gas, which has useful applications, such as denying Mienfoo and Foongus…

110.name: Weezing-Galar
110.overview: With its incredible physical bulk, great typing, and ability, Weezing serves as an excellent physical wall. Its Poison typing gives it a resistance to Fighting-type attacks, making it an incredible check to Fighting-types, such as Virizion and Primeape. Its bulk allows it to easily spread status through Will-O-Wisp…

sets.name: Bulky Support
sets.description:
Set Details
Galarian Weezing is a great disruptive support Pokemon that can support offensive teams by shutting down the abilities that many teams depend on with Neutralizing Gas. Galarian Weezing has a good matchup against teams that rely on Grimmsnarl to take hits, as it is able to disrupt it with Taunt before it…

sets.name: Bulky Support
sets.description: Galarian Weezing benefits its allies by disabling almost all abilities with Neutralizing Gas and spreading poison. It can be used with Pokemon with abilities that cannot be disabled like Calyrex-S and Pokemon with abilities that are not always necessary for them to function such as Iron Hands and Roaring Moon. It…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description: Galarian Weezing combines a fantastic defensive typing, solid defensive stats, and its unique ability to support the OU tier through reliable entry hazard removal. It can use Defog to remove hazards against even Gholdengo due to Neutralizing Gas suppressing Good as Gold. Will-O-Wisp furthers Galarian Weezing's…

sets.name: Stealth Rock
sets.description:
Megahorn can be used over Swords Dance to smash Wo-Chien. Rhydon can invest in Speed to move before slower foes like uninvested Hariyama, though it doesn't gain much from doing so and loses out on some of its bulk this way.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

113.name: Chansey
113.overview: Chansey stands out as the ultimate special wall in the tier, with its gargantuan bulk further boosted by Eviolite allowing it to tank many special attacks from foes like Raikou, Munkidori, Chandelure, and non-Body Press Duraludon. Chansey is a key component on stall teams, as it tanks special moves for its teammates…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description:
Set Details
Seismic Toss prevents Chansey from being completely passive by dealing reliable, consistent damage against non-Ghost-type targets. Heal Bell allows Chansey to cure status ailments for team members like Houndstone. Thunder Wave enables Chansey’s teammates like Basculegion-M and Flamigo to cripple faster…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

sets.name: Little Pink Blob
sets.description: Thanks to its huge special bulk further boosted by Eviolite, Chansey is an extremely effective special wall capable of handling most special attackers in the format, although its passivity almost exclusively limits it to stall teams. Seismic Toss allows Chansey to spread consistent damage, reducing its passivity…

sets.name: Seismic Toss
sets.description:
Shadow Claw is used to secure an OHKO on Flutter Mane and Marshadow. Ice Punch is used to hit Salamence-Mega and Landorus.
Low Kick hits Tyranitar, while Rock Tomb both hits Charizard-Mega-Y and serves as speed control.

123.name: Scyther
123.overview: Swords Dance Scyther is able to fire off powerful Technician-boosted attacks with its great Attack as well as capitalize on the extra bulk it gains from Eviolite, easing setup by gifting it more opportunities. As a pivot, Scyther leverages its great Speed, which lets it outpace top threats in NU like Overqwil, Paldean…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description:
Set Details
Since Close Combat nails Steel-types like Duraludon, Orthworm, and Tera Steel Sylveon, it complements Dual Wingbeat almost perfectly. Trailblaze allows Scyther to boost its Speed, letting it outpace and deal with faster Pokemon such as Kilowattrel, Cinccino, and Inteleon. Since on hyper offense builds…

sets.name: Offensive Pivot
sets.description:
Set Details
U-turn lets Scyther pivot out of bad matchups against Bronzong, Galarian Slowbro, and Duraludon while allowing it to maintain momentum. Close Combat targets the Steel-types that resist and try to switch into Scyther's STAB moves. Swords Dance allows Scyther to take advantage of a free turn, and it lets…

sets.name: Support
sets.description:
Electabuzz works as a good bulky redirector that can redirect away sleep in Spore and Dire Claw while denying Trick Room with Taunt and slowing opposing Pokemon down, utilizing its good Speed.
Electroweb can do decent chip damage while providing speed control, slowing down threats such as Rillaboom and Dragonite.…

sets.name: Trick Room Support
sets.description: Electabuzz is a good Trick Room support Pokemon that uses redirection and disruption, being able to threaten one of the most popular Pokemon in Amoonguss by shutting it down with Taunt and redirecting Spore while also taking hits from threats such as Miraidon. Follow Me is able to redirect powerful attacks like…

sets.name: Bulky Redirector
sets.description:
Magmar’s role is to redirect attacks with Follow Me, allowing its teammates to set up or attack safely.
Vital Spirit lets Magmar redirect status moves like Spore and Sleep Powder from Pokemon such as Amoonguss and Vivillon without being put to sleep. Flame Body can punish certain physical attackers like Rillaboom,…

sets.name: Swords Dance (Bug)
sets.description: Mega Pinsir is a great late-game sweeper and one of Bug's best answers to Fighting-types thanks to its typing and Aerilate ability. Swords Dance boosts its power even further, overpowering foes such as Toxapex and Celesteela. Return is its main Flying-type STAB move, hitting Grass-, Bug-, and Fighting-types such as…

128.name: Tauros-Paldea-Blaze
128.overview: Paldean Tauros-F carves a strong niche in NU thanks to its solid matchup against staples like Alolan Ninetales, Amoonguss, and Incineroar. Paldean Tauros-F poses as a wallbreaker with strong STAB attacks that are hard to switch around. It can also use Bulk Up to increase its power and bulk, making it harder to revenge…

sets.name: FIREFIGHTER (Defensive)
sets.description:
Will-O-Wisp and Intimidate make Paldean Tauros-F a potent check to physical attackers, notably enabling it reliably check Chien-Pao and Kingambit.
Raging Bull: This provides a powerful STAB attack that can also break screens, allowing Paldean Tauros-F’s teammates to clean up more easily.
Body Press Body Press is…

Paldean Tauros-F is fast enough to outspeed key threats such as Mimikyu and Modest Chi-Yu, making an offensive set viable.
Raging Bull versus Flare Blitz: Raging Bull might not be as powerful as Flare Blitz, but its ability to break Reflect and Light Screen give Paldean Tauros-F a unique niche.
Flame Charge versus…

sets.name: Assault Vest
sets.description:
With an Assault Vest, Paldean Tauros-F finds itself a niche checking Choice Band Chien-Pao, Flutter Mane, and Iron Moth.

130.name: Gyarados
130.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out the format page!
If you feel this analysis is missing anything important or needs an update, feel free to suggest any changes here.

sets.name: Rocky Helmet
sets.description:
Gyarados utilizes its unique typing alongside Rocky Helmet to cushion physical sweepers such as Scizor, Great Tusk and Palafin.
Taunt: Taunt forces foes such as Corviknight and Dondozo to attack Gyarados, constraining them to take Rocky Helmet chip while often dealing minimal damage themselves.
Waterfall versus Iron…

sets.name: Physically Defensive (Flying)
sets.description: Gyarados finds a unique spot on Flying-type teams due to its unique combination of Intimidate, ability to spread paralysis, and ability to phaze. Intimidate allows Gyarados to safely switch into physical threats such as Barraskewda and Mamoswine. Dragon Tail punishes setup sweepers such as Weavile, Ceruledge, and…

132.name: Ditto
132.overview: Ditto is a niche yet potent revenge killer, leveraging its Imposter ability to quickly reverse sweeps, which would otherwise pose a significant threat to its team. Choice Scarf is both Ditto's biggest strength and Achilles' heel, allowing it to outspeed the Pokemon it copies; however, it is limited to choosing one…

sets.name: Focus Sash
sets.description:
Set Details
Ditto is uniquely able to punish teams using Restricted Pokemon that threaten opposing species of themselves, and it often serves as a third Restricted Pokemon. Common threats such as Calyrex-S, Zamazenta-C, Miraidon, and Lunala all use STAB moves that hit themselves for super effective damage, making…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Ditto is used for its phenomenal ability to transform into strong opposing Pokemon, which is invaluable in a format with Restricted Pokemon, giving you access to an extra extremely powerful Pokemon. Ditto is a good late-game cleaner that likes copying Pokemon such as Calyrex-S and Miraidon, since it outspeeds and…

134.name: Vaporeon
134.overview: Vaporeon establishes itself as a useful defensive Pokemon, utilizing its ability Water Absorb and the recovery of Wish to slow down great Water-type Pokemon such as Suicune and Basculegion-F. Vaporeon can support its teammates by passing Wish to them and using Flip Turn to bring in strong attackers such as Choice…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Vaporeon establishes itself as a great defensive Pokemon that is able to take on a variety of foes like Volcanion, Empoleon, and Basculegion-F with its ability, Water Absorb, while also Wish passing to its teammates to heal them up. Haze allows Vaporeon to remove stat boosts from setup sweepers such as Reuniclus,…

sets.name: Wish Passer
sets.description:
Set Details
Flip Turn allows Vaporeon to pivot in teammates for free and more easily pass Wish. Alternatively, Roar gives Vaporeon an option to prevent opposing setup sweepers like Nasty Plot Uxie and Calm Mind Sylveon from boosting out of control. However, Haze can be used to avoid Throat Chop blocking sound moves…

135.name: Jolteon
135.overview: Jolteon establishes itself as a premier fast pivot with its great Speed and solid power. Its fast Volt Switch makes it an easy candidate on VoltTurn teams, and its adequate coverage with Alluring Voice and Shadow Ball discourages specially bulky Pokemon like Virizion and Mesprit from switching into it. Jolteon's…

sets.name: Choice Specs
sets.description: Choice Specs boosts Jolteon's decent power to more threatening levels while still leveraging its excellent Speed. Volt Switch allows Jolteon to escape from walls like Cryogonal and Regirock and maintain momentum for its team. Alluring Voice and Shadow Ball are Jolteon's strongest options for coverage against…

sets.name: Lord Helix (Shell Smash Sweeper) (Rock)
sets.description: Omastar finds itself in a valuable role on Rock teams for its potent setup move in Shell Smash, along with STAB Hydro Pump bringing a deluge onto Ground teams as a rare special attacker for the type. It starts rallying its coverage options with Ice Beam, which targets Garchomp and Water Absorb Clodsire as well as…

sets.name: Offensive
sets.description: Mega Aerodactyl's unrivalled Speed tier, attacking prowess, and decent defensive profile make it a great all-around offensive threat in the metagame, outpacing and forcing out a huge portion of faster threats, such as Iron Moth, Galarian Moltres, and Enamorus lacking Choice Scarf. Dual Wingbeat provides a secondary…

sets.name: Dragon Dance
sets.description: Dragon Dance turns Mega Aerodactyl into a threatening sweeper, leveraging its combination of power, Speed, and coverage to great effect. It particularly preys on faster teams that over-rely on using faster speed control options to check most sweepers, such as Choice Scarf users Mienshao and Keldeo. Earthquake nails…

sets.name: Offensive Utility (Flying)
sets.description: By virtue of its great coverage as well as its sky-high Attack and Speed, Mega Aerodactyl finds a great spot on Flying teams as a late-game cleaner. Earthquake pairs well with Stone Edge, threatening the likes of Heatran and Hisuian Goodra and OHKOing Tapu Koko after Stealth Rock damage. Ice Fang is a great coverage…

sets.name: Dragon Dance (Rock)
sets.description: This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

sets.name: CurseLax
sets.description: With Snorlax's Normal typing and Thick Fat, it nets itself a niche as a wall to Chandelure that can also snowball into a dangerous bulky sweeper with Curse. Crunch nails Ghost-types like Chandelure and Basculegion-M as well as Bronzong, Heat Crash allows Snorlax to hit Steel-types like Copperajah and Bronzong, and…

sets.name: Curse
sets.description: This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

144.name: Articuno
144.overview:
Articuno has a unique niche in the metagame thanks strong Ice-type STAB attacks and great bulk. Blizzard allows Articuno to tear through common cores utilizing Rillaboom, Landorus, and Raging Bolt.
Alolan Ninetales is mandatory with Articuno, patching up Blizzard's accuracy and amplifying its bulk and damage output…

sets.name: Choice Specs
sets.description: Blizzard is used as Articuno's primary Ice-type STAB move, as it threatens both foes. Ice Beam is used for a strong single-target attack, and Freeze-Dry is chosen to threaten Water-types like Ogerpon-W, Urshifu-R, and Terastallized Amoonguss, which otherwise take it's hits exceptionally well. Sheer Cold is preferred…

sets.name: Wall
sets.description: Articuno's amazing bulk and reliable recovery make it a solid wall against special attackers like Tatsugiri, Venusaur, and Shaymin in spite of its bad typing, differentiating it from more popular walls like Milotic and Snorlax. Freeze-Dry has the best combination of coverage and power of any of Articuno's attacks,…

sets.name: Specially Defensive (Flying)
sets.description: Articuno's special bulk lets it serve as a special wall on Flying teams, dealing with threatening foes such as Kyurem, Greninja, and Galarian Moltres. Freeze-Dry lets Articuno handle Dragon-, Ground-, and Water-types such as Choice Specs Dragapult, Gastrodon, Landorus, Toxapex, and Primarina. Futhermore, it can be…

145.name: Zapdos-Galar
145.overview: Galarian Zapdos is one of the few Flying-type threats with powerful Flying-type STAB moves that can threaten popular metagame threats like the Ogerpon formes, the Urshifu formes, Rillaboom, and Amoonguss. Its Fighting-type STAB moves paired with Defiant can break through the popular Intimidate users Hisuian Arcanine…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Galarian Zapdos is primarily used as a fast offensive wallbreaker that doesn't need to worry about Intimidate thanks to Defiant. The secondary mode for it is being a fast support Pokemon with Coaching to help a partner improve their offensive and defensive capabilities. Galarian Zapdos matches up particularly well…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Zapdos is a defensive pivot that is able to take hits, spread status, and build momentum for its team. Volt Switch is a strong option for a pivoting move, being both STAB-boosted and non-contact, but U-turn is a solid alternative to avoid being blocked by Ground-types like Gliscor and Ting-Lu. Thunder Wave is a…

sets.name: Black Glasses
sets.description: Galarian Moltres utilizes its great bulk, typing, and movepool to become a great offensive support Pokemon that can also beat the powerful Calyrex formes. Fiery Wrath provides a consistent spread STAB option paired with its chance to flinch, complimenting Galarian Moltres's bulk as a great tool to rack up damage. Foul…

sets.name: Bulky Spread Attacker
sets.description:
Choice Specs Galarian Moltres is amazing at spreading damage across the opposing team, being able to heavily damage most Pokemon in the format.
With Tera Dark's boost, Dark Pulse is a strong option against rain-centered teams, as it can bypass Pelipper's Wide Guard and 2HKO Archaludon. It is also strong and the most…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description: The speed investment allows Moltres-G to outspeed Max Speed Booster Energy Gouging Fire and Choice Scarf Landorus-T in Tailwind.

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Moltres is an excellent defensive Pokemon, using its typing and ability to punish a multitude of physical attackers in the tier, threatening a burn with Flame Body to stop pivoting from U-turn or reliably walling the likes of Zamazenta lacking Stone Edge, Kingambit, Rillaboom, and most Dragonite and Great Tusk sets.…

149.name: Dragonite
149.overview:
Dragonite's great movepool, well-rounded stats, great ability in Inner Focus that makes it immune to Fake Out and Intimidate from Incineroar, and stellar matchups into threats like Urshifu-R, Rillaboom, and Ogerpon-W makes Dragonite a great Pokemon in Regulation F.
When holding a Choice Band, Dragonite becomes one of…

sets.name: Bulky Attacker
sets.description: Dragonite uses its high Attack and strong defenses, which are further boosted by Multiscale, to be a very powerful bulky physical attacker. It can be used to check a variety of metagame threats, including Sneasler, Rillaboom, and Torkoal, and provide support for itself and its teammates through the likes of Tailwind…

sets.name: Choice Band
sets.description:
Extreme Speed is a priority move that hits the majority of the metagame for neutral damage, making it ideal to lock into.
Aerial Ace allows Dragonite to OHKO Pokemon like Urshifu-R, Rillaboom, and Amoongus without having to be lock into Outrage. Aqua Jet is secondary priority and allows it to double up with its…

sets.name: Loaded Dice Sweeper
sets.description:
Loaded Dice Dragonite threatens heavy damage and a boost to its Speed with Scale Shot, allowing it to outspeed Adamant Chien-Pao and Modest Flutter Mane. Inner Focus can be used to stop Incineroar from slowing it down, while Multiscale can make it difficult to remove, letting it get a key Scale Shot off.
Dragonite is…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description: Mewtwo is a niche wallbreaker by courtesy of its access to Nasty Plot and usable Speed stat letting it outspeed Pokemon like Arceus and most Eternatus variants. Its ability to dismantle common defensive Pokemon, like most Arceus formes, and discourage Pokemon like Zacian-C from switching in, makes it a very hard…

sets.name: Choice Specs
sets.description:
Choice Specs Mewtwo is a strong and decently fast wallbreaker that crucially outspeeds Arceus. Its power and coverage give Mewtwo the ability to OHKO or 2HKO just about every Pokemon in the tier.
However, Mewtwo can easily be revenge killed by Miraidon and Koraidon, both of which outspeed and threaten a KO on it after…

sets.name: Mega Y Wallbreaker
sets.description:
Possessing an earth-shattering 194 Special Attack stat, Speed sufficient to outrun most Pokemon in the metagame, and coverage for its checks, Mega Mewtwo Y is a very threatening wallbreaker for balance and bulky offense teams.
After a boost, Psystrike is able to OHKO specially bulky Pokemon such as defensive Primal…

sets.name: Tera Fairy Wallbreaker
sets.description: Mewtwo carves out a niche for itself as a wallbreaker and late-game cleaner on hyper offense teams thanks to its good Speed stat and ability to blow past most defensive Pokemon after a Nasty Plot boost. It especially excels against balance with the right support, as common cores such as Giratina-O + Alomomola and…

151.name: Mew
151.overview: Mew distinguishes itself from other common Psychic-types like Slowbro and Cresselia by virtue of its incredibly deep movepool, which, when combined with its rounded statline, lets it run a variety of viable sets. Mew can take advantage of common utility and defensive Pokemon like Cyclizar and Gastrodon, making…

sets.name: Suicide Lead
sets.description: Mew is a premier entry hazard setter for hyper offense thanks to having both Spikes and Stealth Rock, various utility options, and respectable bulk to get up multiple layers of hazards. Taunt allows Mew to shut down Pokemon attempting to hinder its ability to set hazards or take advantage of its passivity, such as…

sets.name: Spikes Setter
sets.description:
Set Details
Mew sets itself apart from other Spikes users, namely Gastrodon and Chesnaught, thanks to its good Speed stat and incredible utility movepool. Body Press is a solid coverage move that lets Mew invest in bulk while also targeting Cyclizar, one of the tier's most common hazard removers. It also hits the…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description:
Set Details
Nasty Plot Mew stands as one of the most dangerous sweepers in the metagame with its combination of bulk and Speed and amazing coverage options. Psyshock is the STAB move of choice, allowing it to threaten Blissey and Cyclizar immediately after a boost without needing to terastallize. Draining Kiss allows…

157.name: Typhlosion-Hisui
157.overview: Choice Specs Typhlosion is an insanely powerful special attacker in a tier without much in the way of sturdy Fire-resistant Pokemon. Much of its power comes from its move Eruption, which has unparalleled strength with Typhlosion at full health and can OHKO most neutral targets such as Mesprit and Primeape. With…

sets.name: Glass Cannon
sets.description:
Hisuian Typhlosion has one job: spam powerful Fire-type moves.
Eruption is the main STAB attack of choice for Hisuian Typhlosion due to its high Base Power and spread damage, while Heat Wave can be used if Hisuian Typhlosion's health is lowered or at risk of being lowered by a foe's attack. Overheat gives Hisuian…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Hisuian Typhlosion is weaker than Chandelure, meaning Chandelure outclasses it in most roles. However, Hisuian Typhlosion has way better Speed and Focus Blast; this gives it an edge on offense teams that want a faster Choice Scarf user or on builds that prefer a wallbreaker that isn't entirely walled by Incineroar.

160.name: Feraligatr
160.overview: Feraligatr faces heavy competition from fellow physical setup sweepers like Bisharp, Mimikyu, and Necrozma; however, it carves a niche for itself by being more immediately threatening with its ability Sheer Force. Feraligatr also has solid coverage, letting it heavily damage Water-resistant targets like Slowbro and…

sets.name: Setup Sweeper
sets.description:
Set Details
Feraligatr can be a terrifying setup sweeper, either muscling through teams with Swords Dance or shoring up its lacking Speed with Dragon Dance. The STAB move of choice depends on what boosting move it opts to run. If Swords Dance is used, then Aqua Jet is preferred due to its status as a priority move,…

sets.name: Utility (Poison)
sets.description: Crobat finds a solid role on Poison teams thanks to its Ground immunity, high Speed, and solid utility movepool. Brave Bird deals with threats such as Iron Valiant, Mega Gallade, and Ogerpon-W. Infiltrator + Toxic is used to spread status against targets such as Substitute Hydreigon and Kyurem, while U-turn can be…

sets.name: Pivot
sets.description: Chinchou's defensive typing and ability, as well as its excellent coverage, make it a serviceable pivot. It is malleable to many team archetypes due to its role compression; Chinchou can block Volt Switch from common Electric-types like Elekid and opposing Chinchou, switch into Wingull and Fire-types like Hisuian…

184.name: Azumarill
184.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out the format page!
If you feel this analysis is missing anything important or needs an update, feel free to suggest any changes here.

sets.name: Offensive
sets.description:
With a fantastic typing that checks key threats like Chien-Pao and Chi-Yu and a good STAB priority attack, Azumarill is a common choice for a team's bulky Water-type.
Superpower: Superpower often forces Azumarill to switch out, but it deals massive damage to the important Iron Bundle.
Other Options
Ice Spinner: Ice…

sets.name: Belly Drum
sets.description:
Azumarill's excellent typing and access to priority Aqua Jet make it one of the best Belly Drum users in the metagame.
Liquidation: Liquidation remains a strong Water-type STAB move and makes Azumarill more usable in matchups where Belly Drum can't be used. When boosted by Belly Drum, Liquidation is fully capable of…

sets.name: Substitute + Encore
sets.description: This set does not have a full write-up yet. If you'd like to finish this, feel free to reserve it here!

sets.name: Belly Drum
sets.description: Belly Drum Azumarill serves as a phenomenal setup sweeper on hyper offense teams that is able to KO most Pokemon that do not resist Water with +6 Aqua Jet outright or after slight chip, such as Tornadus-T, Weavile, and Cobalion. It is also a dangerous wallbreaker that annihilates slower counterplay. Azumarill is vital…

186.name: Politoed
186.overview: Politoed is an excellent wielder of the Drizzle ability, with good natural bulk to go with its offensive coverage options and utility that serve it well. Drizzle enables the plethora of strong Swift Swim users, such as Barraskewda, Basculegion-F, and Overqwil, while also giving Hurricane users like Kilowattrel and…

sets.name: Perish Trapper
sets.description:
Set Details
Whirlpool is an effective means to trap unsuspecting checks like Blissey, Suicune, and Umbreon, enabling Perish Song to break through these defensive pieces. Ice Beam nails Grass- and Dragon-types like Amoonguss, Chesnaught, and Cyclizar. Encore can disrupt slower Pokemon like Reuniclus and Slowbro and put…

sets.name: Sun Support
sets.description:
Jumpluff is a sun staple, providing fast and invaluable support in the form of speed control and sleep, among others.
Sunny Day is a good option because of Jumpluff's high Speed, letting it reliably override opposing weather before anything moves while boosting the power of allies such as Charizard. Rage Powder is the…

195.name: Quagsire
195.overview: Facing RU with a stalwart expression, Quagsire is a decent physical wall, utilizing its ability Unaware to stonewall very pertinent setup sweepers such as Bisharp and Mimikyu. Quagsire uses Pokemon such as Bisharp and Mimikyu as opportunities to heal off chip damage or establish Spikes, while Toxic allows Quagsire to…

sets.name: Unaware Wall
sets.description: Quagsire is a notable physical wall thanks to its ability Unaware, which allows it to ignore boosts from setup sweepers like Salamence and Scizor. Toxic puts many notable Pokemon in the tier on a timer such as Hippowdon and Gastrodon. Quagsire ideally wants Spikes over Stealth Rock to force more chip damage on…

sets.name: Spikes
sets.description:
Set Details
Toxic allows Quagsire to threaten Flying-types such as Noivern and Yanmega while also helping wear down Pokemon such as Gastrodon and Swampert. Heavy-Duty Boots allows Quagsire to ignore chip damage from entry hazards that might otherwise overwhelm it, while Leftovers accentuates Quagsire's longevity by…

sets.name: Unaware Wall (Ground)
sets.description: Quagsire is a great option for Ground teams due to its physical bulk and Unaware, checking a wide range of physical attackers like Hisuian Samurott, Cetitan, and Choice Scarf Urshifu-R. Stealth Rock or Spikes wears down threats like Greninja, Iron Valiant, and Meowscarada. Toxic allows Quagsire to further wither down…

197.name: Umbreon
197.overview: Umbreon provides tremendous value as a specially defensive wall that can support its teammates through Wish passing and status spreading. Umbreon often provides Wish support to some of the tier's strong Choice item wallbreakers such as Yanmega, Hisuian Goodra, and Krookodile. Umbreon also props up balance and bulky…

sets.name: Support
sets.description: Boasting excellent defenses and a plethora of utility moves, Umbreon finds itself a niche in providing bulky support along with Inner Focus ignoring the ever so common Fake Out from the likes of Incineroar and Rillaboom. Access to Foul Play allows Umbreon to tackle physical threats such as Calyrex-I as well as…

sets.name: Wish Tank
sets.description:
Umbreon's high bulk allows it tank a slew of offensive Pokemon. Alongside excellent utility moves, Umbreon can generate tempo while surviving even surprise Tera-boosted attacks.
Foul Play: In such a physically leaning format, Foul Play is a strong reliable move to chip the opponent's Pokemon. In particular, it…

sets.name: Wish Support
sets.description:
Set Details
Foul Play hits Jirachi and Dragon Dance Necrozma for large chunks of damage. Toxic helps Umbreon be less passive, threatening to secure a big poison on difficult-to-take-down Pokemon such as Slowbro and Wo-Chien. Protect is a valuable move on Umbreon to scout for Choice-locked attacks from wallbreakers…

199.name: Slowking-Galar
199.overview:
Psychic
Slowking finds stiff competition from Slowbro, which is the far superior physical wall that can be a potential wincon with Iron Defense + Body Press. Additionally, Psychic teams greatly wish to have more offensive teammates, which creates issues with fitting Slowking onto these builds. However, Slowking's…

sets.name: AV Tank
sets.description:
Thanks to its great Special Defense, unique typing, and Regenerator, Galarian Slowking functions well as an Assault Vest user.
Assault Vest: While Galarian Slowking has access to Slack Off, Regenerator can provide some recovery if needed, and an Assault Vest increases its Special Defense to titanic levels.
Future…

sets.name: Assault Vest
sets.description: Assault Vest Galarian Slowking can blanket check hard-hitting special attackers like Walking Wake, Iron Valiant, and Enamorus, with Regenerator allowing it to maintain longevity throughout the course of a game. Moreover, the added bulk from Assault Vest means Galarian Slowking can afford to invest into its Special…

205.name: Forretress
205.overview: Forretress is generally outclassed by the majority of Pokemon in the tier, though it can leverage its great physical bulk and diverse movepool to carve out a niche on certain team structures. It is incredibly flexible as an entry hazard setter, capable of running Spikes, Stealth Rock, and Toxic Spikes as needed for…

sets.name: Support
sets.description:
Set Details
Forretress is a solid choice for the support slot on teams, compressing multiple key positions into one teamslot. Spikes allows it to pair with a Stealth Rock setter, like Gastrodon or Rhyperior, to get up multiple layers of entry hazards. Body Press, with the allocated 164 EVs in Defense, threatens an…

sets.name: Hazard Support (Bug)
sets.description: Forretress finds a solid role on Bug teams thanks to its secondary Steel typing and solid support movepool. Spikes provides chip against switch-ins such as Hisuian Arcanine and Gholdengo. Rapid Spin provides support by removing hazards, allowing teammates like Lokix, Slither Wing, and Kleavor to come in safely. Gyro…

207.name: Gligar
207.overview: Gligar brings a unique set of defensive and support utility that gives it a valuable niche in offensive teams. It has a great defensive profile thanks to its solid Eviolite-boosted bulk, its immunity to Earthquake and Volt Switch, and the absence of viable Ice-type Pokemon in the tier. These properties make Gligar a…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description: Spikes is valuable for offense teams when trying to break through the many walls susceptible to them, such as Slowbro, Wo-Chien, and Registeel. Spikes is meant to be combined with a Stealth Rock setter such as Jirachi, Krookodile, or Necrozma. However, if no Stealth Rock setter can find its place in Gligar's team,…

sets.name: Physical Wall
sets.description:
Set Details
Knock Off lets Gligar remove Heavy-Duty Boots from Pokemon like Kilowattrel, Chandelure, and Incineroar, increasing the efficacy of its entry hazards. Alternatively, Toxic allows it to pressure defensive tanks like Vaporeon and Gastrodon. Gligar can run Stealth Rock alongside Spikes, letting it set up…

sets.name: Curse (Ground)
sets.description:
An Impish nature lets Mega Steelix survive Azumarill's Aqua Jet after it has used Belly Drum.
This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

sets.name: Physically Defensive
sets.description: Snubbull fills a valuable role in Little Cup as a reliable Fighting- and Dark-type check through the combination of its pure Fairy typing and ability in Intimidate, allowing it to check common offensive threats such as Mienfoo, Timburr, and Vullaby. Psychic Fangs allows Snubbull to hit Poison-types like Gastly and…

sets.name: Physical Defensive
sets.description: Physically defensive Qwilfish functions as a competent Spikes setter and physical wall because of its typing, which allows it to come onto the field against Florges and Paldean Tauros-W, and utility. Flip Turn allows Qwilfish to act as a slow pivot, giving teammates safe entry points. Pain Split is Qwilfish's primary…

sets.name: Specially Defensive
sets.description: The Speed investment lets Hisuian Qwilfish move before Adamant Golurk. Haze is another option for the last slot, slowing down setup sweepers like Florges. Toxic Spikes may seem appealing, wearing foes down even quicker alongside Spikes, but grounded Poison-types like Galarian Slowbro and Skuntank are too common for…

212.name: Scizor-Mega
212.overview:
Welcome to Battle Stadium Singles Regulation F! This Pokemon has been updated to reflect the new Pokemon that The Indigo Disk has brought!
If you feel that this analysis is missing important information, feel free to leave feedback here.

sets.name: Priority Attacker
sets.description:
The given EV spread allows Mega Scizor to outspeeed Chien-Pao during Tailwind while maximizing its Attack, allocating the remaining EVs in HP.
Mega Scizor's Bullet Punch is one of the metagame's most reliable ways of dealing with Flutter Mane. Bug Bite is Mega Scizor's strongest STAB move, but Close Combat help Mega…

sets.name: Pivot
sets.description:
Thanks to its high Attack, great defensive typing and solid bulk, Scizor is an effective bulky pivot that can also punish faster foes with Technician-boosted Bullet Punch, such as Flutter Mane, Chien-Pao, and Latios.
Knock Off versus Thief: Beyond being a fantastic support move, Knock Off is a great option against…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description:
Scizor's incredible power and good typing make it one of the premier priority sweepers, being able to sweep through huge threats such as Flutter Mane and Chien-Pao!
Close Combat: Close Combat lets Scizor smash through Ursaluna-B, Archaludon, and Ting-Lu after a boost.
Knock Off: Knock Off is Scizor's best bet against…

sets.name: Choice Band
sets.description: Choice Band Scizor is a strong anti-metagame revenge killer and offensive pivot. Brandishing its ever-infamous Technician-boosted Bullet Punch, it is able to check and OHKO relevant Fairy-type foes in Iron Valiant and Enamorus, as well as Kyurem after entry hazard damage, along with scaring off some speedier foes,…

sets.name: Suicide Lead
sets.description:
Shuckle is an extremely passive Pokemon with a poor typing, preventing it from contributing much mid-game. However, thanks to its decent bulk, great ability in Sturdy, and ways to maintain momentum in Final Gambit and Encore, it is an excellent suicide lead for hyper offense teams.
Final Gambit allows Shuckle to…

sets.name: Sticky Web (Rock)
sets.description: Shuckle is an essential member of any hyper offensive Rock team due to its access to Sticky Web, which allows teammates such as Mega Diancie and Terrakion to dominate the Steel and Dark matchups. With Mental Herb, it isn't scared to set up entry hazards in front of common Taunt users such as Deoxys-S and Zeraora.…

214.name: Heracross
214.overview: Heracross offers itself to teams as a strong wallbreaker that can break holes into foes. Its access to Guts allows Heracross to switch in against status attempts from foes like Vaporeon and Amoonguss while giving it an extra power boost. Heracross's above average bulk lets it handle wallbreakers such as Scrafty,…

sets.name: Bulk Up
sets.description: Bulk Up lets Heracross become a threatening setup sweeper and late-game cleaner, capable of taking games with its above-average bulk and great Attack. Knock Off lets Heracross hit Psychic- and Ghost-types such as Galarian Articuno, Munkidori, Chandelure, and Basculegion-M while removing Heavy-Duty Boots from entry…

sets.name: Guts Wallbreaker
sets.description:
Set Details
Knock Off is good coverage against Ghost-types like Chandelure and Decidueye as well as Psychic-types like Galarian Slowbro and Uxie that also threatens Pokemon heavily reliant on their held items like Incineroar, Kilowattrel, and Porygon2. Facade synergizes well with Guts, allowing Heracross to put major…

sets.name: Flame Orb
sets.description: Trailblaze lets Heracross get around ordinary revenge killers such as Rotom-Heat and Choice Scarf Florges. Swords Dance is better if Heracross wishes to focus purely on wallbreaking, allowing it to push past physically bulky foes like Palossand. Megahorn slams walls like Vileplume and Amoonguss without requiring…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description:
Spikes can be used to capitalize on the switches Heracross forces, though Megahorn offers strong coverage against the likes of Galarian Slowbro and Venusaur, which can otherwise blank Heracross.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

215.name: Sneasel
215.overview: With its blisteringly fast Speed and a deadly STAB combination, Sneasel carves a place for itself in ZU as a powerful wallbreaker and cleaner. STAB Knock Off makes Sneasel excellent at forcing progress, especially since the primary Knock Off absorber, Dipplin, fears its Ice-type attacks, and it outruns many already…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description: Sneasel is an effective Swords Dance sweeper in PU thanks to its incredible STAB combination alongside a decent Attack stat, which both let it hit threats such as Espeon, Hisuian Decidueye, and Delphox, and a great Speed tier that allows it to outspeed all of the previously mentioned Pokemon. With Knock Off, Sneasel…

sets.name: Choice Band
sets.description: Despite its NFE status, Sneasel is a real threat in PU due to it being one of the fastest Pokemon in the tier. When holding Choice Band, Sneasel packs a punch with its relatively high Attack stat and can break through even some of the best walls in the tier such as Venusaur and Amoonguss with Triple Axel. Ice Shard…

sets.name: Fast Attacker
sets.description: Ice Shard lets Sneasel pick off weakened foes and faster attackers like Whimsicott and Jolteon as well as dance around priority like Mach Punch from Hitmonchan. Swords Dance lets Sneasel pick up OHKOs on Charizard and Dipplin and gives it an easier time cleaning up weakened teams, while Low Kick lets it threaten…

sets.name: Specially Defensive Wall (Ice)
sets.description: Piloswine is one of Ice teams' best specially defensive walls, capable of filling some of Ice teams' biggest roles of being an entry hazard setter and Electric immunity. Thick Fat coupled with Eviolite gives Piloswine tremendous bulk, with which it can check dangerous Fire-type threats to Ice such as Volcarona and…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Galarian Corsola walls Choice Specs Kyurem, Nidoking, Mega Diancie, and Mega Charizard Y with its Eviolite-boosted defenses and provides much-needed entry hazards in Stealth Rock for Ghost teams. Night Shade provides Galarian Corsola with a reliable form of damage to threaten these special attackers. Paired with…

sets.name: Water Absorb (Flying)
sets.description: This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Skarmory's great bulk and strong defensive typing let it check common offensive physical threats such as Kingambit, Great Tusk, and Dragonite as well as providing opportunities to set up multiple layers of Spikes. Skarmory is able to set up Iron Defense on physical attackers, preventing it from being overwhelmed and…

sets.name: Mixed Defense
sets.description: Skarmory leverages its incredible defensive typing and physical bulk to act as an effective entry hazard setter and blanket check to physical attackers like Excadrill, Lokix, and Ogerpon. Body Press lets it target the aforementioned Excadrill as well as Scizor and Tyranitar, preventing them from using it as setup…

sets.name: Wallbreaker
sets.description: Thanks to Nasty Plot, an excellent offensive typing, and respectable Speed tier, Houndoom is a terrifying wallbreaker and late-game cleaner in the metagame. Its STAB combination is only resisted by itself, Poliwrath, and the rare Dachsbun, which makes it difficult to switch into once it sets up. Substitute lets…

230.name: Kingdra
230.overview:
Water
Kingdra is a solid Swift Swim special attacker for Water teams; however, it is only functional in this role and faces competition from Basculegion-F, which has superior Special Attack. Along with that, rain teams can opt to not run a special attacking Swift Swim threat and instead choose to run Choice Specs…

233.name: Porygon2
233.overview: Porygon2 is one of the bulkiest Pokemon in the format with Eviolite, which allows it to set up Trick Room for it and its partners and beat most Pokemon in the format one-on-one with Recover. With a Download Special Attack boost, it is able to OHKO Pokemon such as the Landorus formes, Tornadus, and Dragonite with Ice…

sets.name: Offensive Trick Room Setter
sets.description: Porygon2’s primary role is to absorb hits, set up Trick Room, and exert offensive pressure while Trick Room is active. With Eviolite, Porygon2 becomes one of the bulkiest Pokemon in the format, and its access to Recover allows it to heal and remain difficult to remove. Additionally, the Download boost helps it gain…

sets.name: Offensive TR Setter
sets.description: Tera Blast is used as strong Normal-type STAB move that offers coverage once Porygon2 Terastallizes. When Tera Blast is paired with a Download Attack boost, Tera Blast becomes a physical move when Porygon2 Terastallizes. This allows you to hit Assault Vest users like Raging Bolt and Rillaboom harder, as well as…

sets.name: Trick Room
sets.description: This EV Spread survives two Deoxys-A Expanding Forces in Psychic Terrain, as well as two Iron Hands Drain Punches.

sets.name: Immortal Duck
sets.description:
With its wide movepool and impressive bulk courtesy of Eviolite, Porygon2 is a great defensive option.
Tri Attack vs Tera Blast: Tri Attack offers Porygon2 consistent damage and the chance of inflicting status, while Tera Blast allows Porygon2 to make use of Tera to damage foes it might miss out on with Tri Attack…

sets.name: Disruptive Support
sets.description:
Set Details
Smeargle uses its wide movepool to support its offensive teammates. It is the fastest Spore user in the format, and it can shut down foes such as Calyrex-I and Raging Bolt before they can move. Its Moody ability can punish the opponent for not KOing it quickly enough, and a possible Speed boost will allow…

sets.name: Support
sets.description: Smeargle's access to almost every move with Sketch grants it a great niche in Regulation G as a support Pokemon. Follow Me allows Smeargle to protect its more offensive partners, and Spore threatens to shut down opposing Pokemon, making it easier for Smeargle's partners to set up and sweep. Wide Guard blocks the…

sets.name: Hazard Setter
sets.description:
Silk Trap is chosen over similar moves like Burning Bulwark for its ability to slow down foes like Paldean Tauros-W and Scyther; this gives Smeargle an easier time setting entry hazards or denying Defog attempts.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

sets.name: Rapid Spin
sets.description:
Hitmontop is unviable in PU, as it is generally outclassed as a Fighting-type by other Pokemon such as Paldean Tauros-F, Hariyama, and Pawmot and as a form of offense entry hazard control by the likes of Tatsugiri and Scyther.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

sets.name: Rapid Spin
sets.description: Hitmontop is a solid offensive entry hazard remover for its uncommon access to Rapid Spin, solid matchups against spinblockers such as Spiritomb and Trevenant, and high threat level from Close Combat and Technician-boosted Triple Axel. Triple Axel threatens checks to Hitmonotp like Whimsicott and Trevenant, and it…

sets.name: Offensive Pivot
sets.description: Elekid is an elite pivot in Little Cup, with an unmatched Speed tier and a wide range of coverage options that threaten much of the metagame. Vital Spirit provides teams another option to switch in on Spore from Foongus. Elekid's moveset is highly customizable to fit the role it plays on a team. Thunderbolt threatens…

242.name: Blissey
242.overview:

sets.description: With its staggering special bulk, Blissey is the greatest wall to most special attackers in the tier. Thanks to Natural Cure, it also acts as a solid status absorber, preventing the likes of Pecharunt's Malignant Chain, Galarian Slowking's Toxic, and Dragapult's Will-O-Wisp from ruining it permanently. 243.name: Raikou
243.overview: Raikou makes for a powerful sweeper and pivot, utilizing its great Special Attack that is further powered up by Calm Mind and Choice Specs. Set Details
Scald is an excellent coverage move on Raikou, melting Ground-types like Krookodile and Rhyperior while also threatening to burn common checks like Cyclizar and Umbreon. Aura Sphere allows Raikou to hit Dark-, Normal-, and Steel-types like Bisharp, Cyclizar, and Hisuian Goodra for considerable damage.…

sets.name: Calm Mind
sets.description:
Set Details
Calm Mind, in conjunction with Pressure, allows Raikou to become an excellent setup sweeper that can hit hard immediately while also handling opposing Calm Mind threats like Reuniclus and Suicune. Scald lets Raikou take on Ground-types like Krookodile and Rhyperior, as well as punish walls like Cyclizar…

sets.name: Calm Mind
sets.description:
Set Details
Volt Switch allows Raikou to keep up momentum against and chip common switch-ins like Tsareena, Altaria, and Wo-Chien. Scald hits Electric-immune foes like Flygon, Swampert, and Kilowattrel while threatening with burns. Shadow Ball, often boosted by Tera Ghost, has solid neutral coverage, but Thunderbolt…

244.name: Entei
244.overview: Entei acts as one of RU's deadliest wallbreakers, boasting a high Attack stat and an incredible STAB move in Sacred Fire. Flare Blitz can be used as a more powerful and accurate alternative…

Snarl slows down strong special attackers such as Flutter Mane, Raging Bolt, and Iron Crown. Tera Blast can be used instead when using Tera Grass to hit Water-types.

sets.name: Bulky Attacker

Access to strong priority and an ability that prevents Intimidate cycling give Entei a strong niche over other bulky Fire-types like Incineroar and Gouging Fire!
Sacred Fire: Sacred Fire's stupendous burn rate makes switching physical attackers into Entei a terrifyingly risky prospect!

245.overview: Suicune is one of the prominent wincons of the tier. With a great defensive typing in Water and a high base HP stat, Suicune can set up on most bulky Pokemon in the tier. After one Calm Mind, Suicune is tough to revenge kill, as it deters physical attackers from switching in due to a potential burn from Scald, and…

sets.name: Bulky Support
sets.description: With exceptional bulk, a great defensive typing, and Inner Focus allowing it to ignore Fake Out from the likes of Incineroar and Rillaboom, Suicune provides bulky support options thanks to its plethora of utility and disruptive moves. Snarl weakens special attackers like Calyrex-S, Terapagos, and Kyogre. Tailwind…

Set Details
This set focuses on Suicune being a wincon by making use of Pressure and its HP and Speed stats. Substitute + Calm Mind ensures Suicune can set up on bulky but offensively weak Pokemon such as Slowbro, Gastrodon, and Wo-Chien. Protect is excellent for scouting moves on Choice-locked Pokemon, such as…

Boasting excellent defenses and a powerful Attack stat with decent coverage options, Tyranitar functions as a weather-setting tank thanks to its ability, checking meta threats such as Pelipper, Volcarona, and Kingambit.

250.name: Ho-Oh
250.overview: Thanks to Ho-Oh's amazing stats, typing, and access to Regenerator, it cements itself as one of the best Pokemon in AG, making it a very good addition to balance and bulky offense teams. sets.description:
Set Details
Ho-Oh's amazing bulk, great ability, and solid defensive typing let it stand in the way of many offensive threats in the format and support its team with Tailwind. It's far from passive, though; it has great Attack and two excellent STAB moves, meaning that it often wins damage trades against frailer…

sets.name: Nasty Plot (Grass)

260.name: Swampert
260.overview: Swampert distinguishes itself from other bulky Water-types thanks to the combination of its wide utility movepool and Ground typing that leaves it with only one weakness, letting it compress roles for offensive teams. Additionally, its solid bulk lets it function as a blanket check to physical threats like Bisharp and…

sets.name: Support Pivot
sets.description:
Thanks to its bulk and unique dual typing, Swampert is a good Stealth Rock lead that matches up well against most Archaludon sets.
Flip Turn: Unlike Ting-Lu and Hippowdon, Swampert can use Flip Turn to safely bring in its teammates.

Set Details
Swampert is able to leverage its good defensive typing and bulk in order to act as a solid Stealth Rock setter for a variety of teams. Knock Off lets Swampert punish Pokemon reliant on Heavy-Duty Boots that attempt to switch in, such as Noivern and Galarian Zapdos. Flip Turn allows Swampert to act as a…

Set Details
Flip Turn allows Swampert to pivot out and bring in teammates to make progress. Knock Off supplies utility against foes that Swampert cannot hit well with its STAB moves, and it cripples item-reliant foes like Munkidori, Duraludon, and Avalugg. Pelipper utilizes Rocky Helmet to provide useful chip damage against Fake Out users, Ice Spinner Chien-Pao, and…

282.overview: Gardevoir graces RU with a strong offensive typing and good utility in Trace, Trick, and Healing Wish, making it one of the tier's premier Choice Scarf users. Very few Pokemon in the tier are able to come in on its STAB combination, and the few that do, like Registeel and Hisuian Goodra, often find themselves crippled…

The given EV spread allows Mega Gardevoir to survive Shadow Ball from Flutter mane while still able to outspeed scarfed Chi-Yu during Tailwind, allocating the remaining EVs into Special Attack.
Psyshock will deal greater damage to Flutter Mane, but Expanding Force is preferred if Indeedee-F or Tapu Lele is on the same…

286.name: Breloom
286.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out this page!
If you feel this analysis is missing anything important or needs an update, feel free to suggest any changes here.

sets.name: Offensive

Since it can sleep foes without restriction, Breloom is a monstrous foe that applies serious pressure at Team Preview!
Bulldoze: This is Breloom's best option against Gholdengo and provides valuable speed control.

Set Details
Breloom's unique defensive profile makes it a great check to the tier's Dark-types like Krookodile, Umbreon, and Wo-Chien as well as Rhyperior, and it's a decent wallbreaker with good resilience. It's also able to switch into the many Scalds and Thunder Waves in the tier after it activates Poison Heal. In…

297.name: Hariyama
297.overview: Hariyama stands out in NU as a powerful wallbreaker and can double as a devastating sweeper or as a reliable tank. It utilizes Guts to punish status-inflicting moves from many common defensive Pokemon such as Bellibolt, Vaporeon, and Altaria. With access to Drain Punch, Hariyama can keep itself healthy by capitalizing…

With threats such as Chi-Yu, Iron Moth, Volcarona, and Chien-Pao around, Hariyama carves a niche for itself as an Assault Vest user capable of taking them on. Along with its decent bulk, Thick Fat gives it key resistances to Fire- and Ice-type attacks.
Bullet Punch: Bullet Punch provides Hariyama with a…

Thanks to Guts, Hariyama can work as a status absorber and boost its high Attack at the same time, making it a threat to Toxic Spikes-stacking teams.

Set Details
Drain Punch allows Hariyama to sustain itself over the course of the battle, helping versus powerful Steel-types like Duraludon. sets.description: Sableye is a good pick for Dark teams thanks to its Fighting immunity, spinblocking prowess, and ability to pester opposing teams with a wide array of status moves. Night Shade provides a consistent form of damage, preventing Sableye from being too passive, as well as letting it deal with Skarmory and Iron Hands…

Additionally, its ability to spread status with Will-O-Wisp enables the team to handle threatening physical…

What sets Mega Mawile apart from other wallbreakers is the immediate power it gains from Huge Power, meaning…

Ice Punch targets foes like Landorus-T and Gliscor, with the former not being a reliable switch-in at all, as Ice Punch OHKOes it even after Intimidate. Thunder…

sets.name: All-Out Attacker

sets.description: This is a sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

324.name: Torkoal
324.overview:
Torkoal is a premier offensive Pokemon seen on Trick Room teams thanks to its great STAB moves, fantastic ability, and good defenses. Its low Speed also makes it faster than most of the tier when in Trick Room, and it has great matchups into Pokemon like Amoonguss
Some of Torkoal's strongest partners are Trick Room…

This Torkoal set is used for setting up sun and providing additional support for its partners.

Eruption deals powerful Fire-type spread damage; meanwhile, Heat Wave provides consistent spread damage regardless of Torkoal's remaining HP. Weather Ball deals strong, consistent damage while sun is active, and…

Rapid Spin and Stealth Rock grant Torkoal excellent role compression as both an entry hazard remover and setter that can threaten other common leads like Skarmory, Ribombee, and Iron Treads with Lava Plume while also being…

Stone Edge completes its EdgeQuake coverage, letting Trapinch hit Flying-types like Vullaby and…

sets.name: Trapper
sets.description: This is a winning set from the Monotype Rising sets project.

330.overview: Flygon is one of the most versatile Pokemon in the metagame, being able to serve as a wallbreaker, revenge killer, setup sweeper, or entry hazard setter depending on the needs of its team. Its phenomenal STAB coverage and access to U-turn allow it to threaten a majority of the metagame with strong neutral attacks and…

Scale Shot improves Flygon's ability to act as an offensive threat and cleaner. U-turn enables it to deal decent damage to and pivot out of its defensive checks like Slowbro and…

sets.name: Choice Band

Stone Edge threatens Flying-types like Talonflame and Gyarados for super effective damage. U-turn allows Flygon to maintain momentum and pivot out of defensive switch-ins like Slowbro and Chesnaught. A Choice…

334.name: Altaria

Set Details

Alternatively, Altaria can use Haze to prevent setup sweepers like Arcanine and Incineroar from taking advantage of it. Roar works similarly, and it can rack up entry hazard damage on foes, though it's worse in…

Pixilate-boosted Return checks the aforementioned Dragon-types as well as Fighting- and Dark-types such as Terrakion, Mega Sableye, and Mandibuzz.…

342.name: Crawdaunt
342.overview: The big bad crawdad of competitive Pokemon, Crawdaunt remains a relevant wallbreaker and stall dismantler several generations after its debut. Set Details
