Aqua Jet ensures an already set up Crawdaunt can fire off strong attacks before being revenge killed, preventing faster Pokemon…

Set Details

Aqua Jet functions as a STAB priority attack that allows Crawdaunt to overcome its relative slowness, picking up valuable revenge kills and chip damage on faster threats like Gengar,…

373.name: Salamence

376.overview: Metagross serves as a good defensive Pokemon on balance teams thanks to its great defenses that are bolstered even further by Assault Vest, its good ability, and its nice defensive typing against foes like Raging Bolt and Iron Crown. Its access to coverage and high Attack stat make it appreciated as an offensive…

Leftovers keeps it healthy throughout the game, with Protect providing additional turns of recovery.
Tera Water is the standard Tera Type for Registeel, allowing it to resist…

Set Details

sets.description: Skuntank's unique typing, ability in Aftermath, and movepool tailored for both stallbreaking and revenge killing establish it as a solid glue Pokemon in the metagame for a variety of team structures. sets.name: Specially Defensive (Poison)
sets.description: Skuntank fills a very important role on Poison teams by packing a much-desired Psychic immunity, which allows it to switch into Psychic-type moves from many common threats such as Greninja, Flutter Mane, and Hatterene. It proceeds to force them out with its hard hitting Poison Jab-which can spread poison around, while…

437.name: Bronzong
437.overview: Bronzong is an elite Steel-type in NU thanks to both its great mixed bulk and an excellent ability in Levitate, which, unlike other Steel-types, allows it to check many Ground-types like Flygon and Gligar, along with other Pokémon that rely on Ground-type coverage to hit Steel-types like Diancie. Bronzong is…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description:
Set Details
Body Press pairs well with Bronzong's good Defense, letting it threaten Dark- and Steel-types like Incineroar, Scrafty, and Copperajah. Earthquake lets Bronzong target Fire- and Poison-types like Chandelure and Galarian Slowbro, and even after Intimidate, it hits Overqwil harder than Body Press can. Heavy…

sets.name: Bulky Setup Sweeper
sets.description:
Set Details
Body Press lets Bronzong pressure Steel- and Dark-types like Copperajah, Klefki, and Incineroar. Stored Power lets it more easily break through Fighting-resistant and -immune Pokemon that try to switch in like Sylveon, Vileplume, and Decidueye, while the scaling power from both Bronzong's boosted offenses…

sets.name: Iron Defense (Psychic)
sets.description:
Will never be OHKOed from full HP by an unboosted Chien-Pao's Crunch.
This is a sample set as we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

445.name: Garchomp
445.overview:
Welcome to Battle Stadium Singles Regulation F! This Pokemon has been updated to reflect the new Pokemon that The Indigo Disk has brought!
If you feel that this analysis is missing important information, feel free to leave feedback here.

sets.name: Glass Cannon
sets.description:
Garchomp utilizes its high Attack and good Speed to outspeed and greatly weaken foes with powerful spread damage.
Using both Stomping Tantrum and Earthquake allows Garchomp to switch between dealing single-target and spread damage. Earthquake, however, is used most effectively when Garchomp's partner is able to use…

sets.name: Sweeper
sets.description:
Thanks to its good bulk, unique Speed tier, and excellent use of Terastallization, Garchomp is a solid offensive threat.
Scale Shot: While the Defense drop is troublesome against priority users like Chien-Pao, the Speed boost makes Garchomp far more difficult to revenge kill overall.
Iron Head: While Iron Head only…

sets.name: Entry Hazard Setter
sets.description:
While Garchomp is known for its offensive prowess, its access to incredible tools like Rough Skin, Dragon Tail, and a good Speed stat make it a viable alternative to Archaludon and Ting-Lu for setting entry hazards and phazing.
Dragon Tail: Dragon Tail has marvelous synergy with setting up hazards, making it the prime…

sets.name: All-Out Attacker
sets.description:
Garchomp's fantastic Speed tier and power means it doesn't have to set up to be dangerous.
Scale Shot: Choice Band appreciates the Speed boost, giving Garchomp another option for late-game cleaning. Even on Choice Scarf sets, Scale Shot is a less risky Dragon-type attack over Outrage, and outspeeding extremely fast…

448.name: Lucario
448.overview: By virtue of its access to the rare Extreme Speed paired with Swords Dance and a decent offensive typing, Lucario carves out a small niche as a setup sweeper that can revenge kill faster threats like Maushold and Shift Gear Toxtricity. Its ample coverage also makes it difficult for defensive staples like Jirachi and…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description:
Set Details
Lucario's access to the elusive combination of Swords Dance and Extreme Speed makes it a terrifying sweeper and revenge killer in the right circumstances. Meteor Mash serves as a powerful, if somewhat inaccurate, STAB move with the occasional Attack boost being a nice bonus that can further bolster…

sets.name: Swords Dance (Fighting)
sets.description: This is the sample set before we work on new analyses. This will be updated as the metagame continues to develop. Stay tuned!

450.name: Hippowdon
450.overview:
Welcome to Battle Stadium Singles Regulation F! This Pokemon has been updated to reflect the new Pokemon that The Indigo Disk has brought!
If you feel that this analysis is missing important information, feel free to leave feedback here.

sets.name: Stealth Rock Support
sets.description:
Hippowdon remains a solid Stealth Rock setter, distinguishing itself from Ting-Lu with access to Yawn and consistent recovery.
Sand Stream: The passive damage caused from Sandstorm is excellent for breaking Focus Sashes and Multiscale on Dragonite, but be careful, as the same applies to your own team if Hippowdon…

sets.name: Tank
sets.description: Hippowdon's natural mixed bulk and access to recovery allow it to be a useful defensive piece of sand balance teams, checking numerous threats such as Excadrill, Thundurus-T, and Okidogi. Hippowdon is also an effective Stealth Rock setter, especially due to its matchup against the most common entry hazard removal in…

sets.name: Sand Support
sets.description: Hippowdon is a mandatory pick on sand-based Ground teams for its access to Sand Stream, which allows it to enable one of the type's most dangerous offensive threats in Sand Rush Excadrill; in addition, its amazing bulk lets it further support its team as a mixed wall and entry hazard setter. Earthquake is the default…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Hippowdon is a capable defensive Ground-type that utilizes its impressive mixed bulk and useful utility movepool to stand in the way of Pokemon such as Tapu Koko, Volcarona, and Zamazenta. While it competes for a teamslot with other defensive Ground-types that generally offer more utility and are less passive,…

454.name: Toxicroak
454.overview: Toxicroak stands out for having the rare combination of Dry Skin and a great typing both offensively and defensively, which makes it the premier switch-in to Water-types like Vaporeon, Milotic, Paldean Tauros-W, Basculegion, and Inteleon. Toxicroak is also less susceptible to being revenge killed after it has set up;…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description:
Set Details
Close Combat is the preferred STAB option, allowing Toxicroak to 2HKO defensive walls like Alolan Muk and Klefki with Stealth Rock. Alternatively, Drain Punch helps provide more longevity against foes like Alolan Muk and Swampert, and it can counteract Life Orb chip if needed. Sucker Punch helps Toxicroak…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description:
Set Details
Dark Pulse hits Psychic- and Ghost-types like Galarian Slowbro, Chandelure, and Basculegion that would resist or be immune to its STAB attacks. Vacuum Wave can be used over Dark Pulse, allowing Toxicroak to KO revenge killers like Porygon-Z, Flygon, and Inteleon. A Timid nature allows Toxicroak to outspeed…

460.name: Abomasnow
460.overview: With the combination of Aurora Veil, Snow Warning, and a strong offensive profile, Abomasnow is ZU's sole viable snow setter and a threatening offensive tank. The Defense boost from snow even helps Abomasnow tank attacks like Hitmontop's Close Combat and Lycanroc's Stone Edge. Abomasnow's snow pairs well with Beartic,…

sets.name: Aurora Veil
sets.description: Abomasnow is a chilling offensive tank courtesy of its offensive typing and the combination of Snow Warning and Blizzard. Giga Drain provides consistent recovery for Abomasnow and hits Water-types like Clawitzer and Poliwrath, though Leaf Storm can be used for immediate burst damage at the cost of lowering its…

sets.name: Sailor Snow (Aurora Veil Support) (Grass)
sets.description: Abomasnow fulfills a unique niche on Grass teams as the best dual screens setter that the type has access to. With Aurora Veil and its ability Snow Warning, it can significantly decrease the amount of damage common setup sweepers on Grass teams take, such as Sinistcha and the Ogerpon formes. Blizzard's perfect…

sets.name: Hail Setter (Ice)
sets.description: Thanks to its ability to set snow and Aurora Veil, Abomasnow is a valuable choice for Ice-type teams. Its Grass typing provides a way to deal with Water-types such as Azumarill, Basculegion, and Quagsire. Giga Drain is its main STAB move, dealing damage while providing recovery for Abomasnow. Alternatively, it can use…

sets.name: Boots Attacker
sets.description: Weavile is an excellent wallbreaker thanks to its high Speed and Attack and high-Base Power moves, including a Knock Off that Skarmory, Gliscor, and Clefable cannot safely absorb. Weavile also provides excellent speed control for teams, outspeeding the entire unboosted metagame besides Darkrai, Zamazenta, Dragapult,…

sets.name: Choice Band
sets.description: With Choice Band, an excellent STAB combination, high Speed, and an excellent attacking stat, Weavile is a powerful wallbreaker that can act as a revenge killer with Ice Shard and generate progress with Knock Off. Weavile is also able to threaten common Knock Off absorbers such as Clefable, Gliscor, and even…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description: After a Swords Dance, Weavile transforms into extremely speedy threat that deals huge damage very quickly due to its already high Attack stat and a Speed stat that outspeeds almost any other foe in the metagame. Triple Axel lets Weavile knock out foes such as Tornadus-T and Hydrapple and, thanks to its ability to hit…

sets.name: Swords Dance (Dark)
sets.description: Weavile is an incredible wallbreaker on Dark teams because of its high Speed and access to Swords Dance. At +2, Triple Axel allows Weavile to break through defensive Pokemon such as Great Tusk, Swampert, and specially defensive Corviknight while also letting it OHKO offensive ones like the Ogerpon formes and Walking…

462.name: Magnezone
462.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out this page!
If you feel this analysis is missing anything important or needs an update, feel free to suggest any changes here.

sets.name: Pivot
sets.description:
Magnezone stands out as one of the best pivots in the metagame, having the tools to deal with dangerous threats such as Iron Bundle, Flutter Mane, Dondozo, and Corviknight.
Mirror Coat: If using Sturdy, Mirror Coat can get a shocking KO on powerful Special Attackers that Magnezone struggles to KO, such as Chi-Yu and…

sets.name: Choice Specs
sets.description: Choice Specs Magnezone is a high-voltage wallbreaker that takes advantage of the lack of special tanks in RU to blast holes in teams. Volt Switch lets it keep momentum flowing against switch-ins like Hisuian Goodra, Wo-Chien, and Umbreon. Tri Attack Analytic lets Flash Cannon OHKO Krookodile while also amping up Volt…

sets.name: Assault Vest
sets.description: Assault Vest Magnezone uses its typing that is filled with key resistances to keep in check many of RU's special attackers and walls, such as Gardevoir, Jirachi, and Slowbro, and turn them into opportunities to fire off its Analytic-boosted STAB moves. Discharge is chosen over Thunderbolt for the increased paralysis…

sets.name: Assault Vest (Electric)
sets.description: Equipped with an Assault Vest, Magnezone turns into a bulky tank on Electric teams. With its bulk, it is able to serve as the type's best answer to strong Fairy-types such as Hatterene and Flutter Mane. Flash Cannon allows Magnezone to threaten Fairy- and Ice-types such as Hatterene, Clefable, and Alolan Ninetales.…

464.name: Rhyperior
464.overview: Rhyperior is an excellent Pokemon, being able to set Stealth Rock, take hits, and deal damage in return. Rhyperior's typing and bulk allow it to stand up against many threats in the tier, including Entei, Mimikyu, and Maushold, while its powerful STAB EdgeQuake can overwhelm Pokemon like Bisharp, Jirachi, and Noivern.…

sets.name: Stealth Rock
sets.description: Dragon Tail can be used to phaze opponents and rack up entry hazard chip damage with Rhyperior's Stealth Rock, and Rock Blast is an alternative to Stone Edge that can be run for its higher accuracy.

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description: Substitute can also be run as Rhyperior's last move to more easily set up. The second EV set allows Swords Dance Rhyperior to OHKO Bronzong with a +2 Temper Flare after Stealth Rock damage and outspeed bulky foes like Vaporeon and Sylveon with the rest of the EVs poured into HP to maximize bulk.

sets.name: All-out Attacker
sets.description: The Speed EVs allows Rhyperior to outpace Vaporeon.

sets.name: Physically Defensive
sets.description: Sporting good physical bulk and Regenerator, Tangrowth can act as a great check to a lot of physical attackers like Urshifu-R, Landorus-T, and Great Tusk, which struggle to deal significant damage to it and get chipped via Rocky Helmet. Giga Drain helps Tangrowth stay healthy and lets it win one-on-one against Pokemon…

sets.name: Physically Defensive
sets.description: Tangrowth's excellent natural physical bulk, crucial resistances, and variety of utility options at its disposal come together to make it a solid physical wall, being an effective stop to the likes of Tyranitar and Excadrill. Regenerator only serves to further complement the aforementioned defensive traits, blessing…

sets.name: Assault Vest
sets.description: Tangrowth is a solid special wall that can check threats like Enamorus, Hydreigon, Keldeo, and Alakazam thanks to Assault Vest and Regenerator, allowing it to pivot against these threats reliably throughout the game. Tangrowth's great movepool also allows it to punish specific targets to help its team; for example,…

sets.name: All-out Attacker
sets.description: Magmortar finds a place in the tier as a dangerous special attacker with a plethora of good coverage moves. Scorching Sands lets Magmortar hit other Fire-types such as Typhlosion and Camerupt as well as Rock-types like Regirock and Lycanroc. Thunderbolt damages Flying-types like Drifblim and Water-types like…

469.name: Yanmega
469.overview: Yanmega finds a niche on Bug teams thanks to Tinted Lens, which turns Yanmega into a fantastic wallbreaker. This lets Yanmega deal with Steel- and Flying-types such as Archaludon and Gliscor that would otherwise pose a threat to Bug teams. However, Yanmega is held back by its typing, mediocre general bulk, and a…

sets.name: Choice Specs (Bug)
sets.description: Yanmega serves as a potent wallbreaker in Bug teams, as Tinted Lens allows it to bypass many of the common checks to the type. Bug Buzz hits hard against most of the tier, 2HKOing key foes such as Toxapex, Gliscor, and Archaludon. Air Slash deals with Pokemon that resist Bug Buzz even through Tinted Lens such as…

472.name: Gliscor
472.overview: This Pokemon has been updated for the return of the Regulation G format and its powerful restricted threats!

sets.name: Glis
sets.description:
With its great typing and incredible longevity thanks to Poison Heal, Gliscor is a frustratingly potent defensive threat that may be the single largest cause of matches being determined by the match timer running out!
Toxic: Gliscor is one of few Pokemon to be given Toxic via TM, allowing it to put opposing walls on a…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description: Facade takes advantage of Gliscor's Poison Heal to give it a strong neutral attack against common foes like Ogerpon-W, Zapdos, Dragonite, and Alomomola. Knock Off allows it to force progress early-game, crippling many manner of foes like Dondozo, Ting-Lu, Zapdos, and Terastallized Garganacl while giving it the…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description: Gliscor can set multiple layers of Spikes throughout a game, making it the preferred entry hazard to run, but it can also serve as a good Stealth Rock setter for offensive teams that aren't reliant on stacking multiple entry hazards to force progress or teams that prefer a different Spiker instead. Knock Off most…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Due to Gliscor's diverse defensive capabilities and extensive utility movepool, it cements itself as a top-tier pick on stall teams. Toxic helps Gliscor put setup sweepers like Swords Dance Koraidon and Extreme Killer Arceus on a timer while also crippling defensive Pokemon like Ho-Oh and defensive Arceus formes.…

sets.name: Substitute
sets.description: Mamoswine functions as an excellent wallbreaker thanks to its nearly unwallable Ice / Ground STAB combination and high base Attack, allowing it to break through common threats such as Hydrapple, Heatran, Toxapex, and Skeledirge. Ice Shard is a reliable priority move that allows Mamoswine to bypass its below average…

sets.name: Choice Scarf (Ice)
sets.description: Choice Scarf Mamoswine is a specialized niche option for Ice teams, being just fast enough to provide relevant speed control, notably just outspeeding Choice Specs Dragapult with a Jolly nature while helping shore up the awful Fire matchup with fast and strong EdgeQuake coverage. In addition, it offers nice role…

sets.name: Choice Band (Ground)
sets.description: Mamoswine is a great offensive option for Ground teams thanks to its huge Attack stat, access to priority, and unique STAB combination. Thick Fat's extremely valuable Ice resistance allows Mamoswine to switch into foes like Articuno and Alolan Ninetales. Icicle Crash shreds Grass-type Pokemon, such as Amoonguss and…

474.name: Porygon-Z
474.overview: Glitching its way into SV RU, Porygon-Z acts as a niche, yet deadly, setup sweeper and wallbreaker. Its monstrous Special Attack stat combined with Download and Agility lets Porygon-Z clean up weakened teams, while its Normal typing lets it maintain a reliable STAB move with Tera Blast, even if it needs to…

sets.name: Agility
sets.description: An impressive Special Attack and good coverage land Porygon-Z a niche in RU as a ferocious wallbreaker and cleaner. Agility lets Porygon-Z outpace everything in the unboosted metagame, including Choice Scarf users like Krookodile and Mienshao. Ice Beam nails Ground-types like Hippowdon, Flying-types like Galarian…

sets.name: Z-Conversion Sweeper
sets.description: Porygon-Z is among Normal's best avaliable setup sweepers due to its coveted access to Z-Conversion. Shadow Ball, when on the first moveslot, ensures Porygon-Z turns into a Ghost-type upon using Z-Conversion. After Z-Conversion, Adaptability-boosted Shadow Ball hits insanely hard, OHKOing the likes of Archaludon, Tapu…

475.name: Gallade
475.overview:
Gallade is a strong option on Trick Room teams thanks to its high physical damage output, unique coverage and utility movepool. It can deal with many problematic matchups for Trick Room, such as set up sweepers via Sacred Sword and spread moves via Wide Guard.
Gallade is used on teams with Indeedee-F, as the…

sets.name: Offensive Trick Room Sweeper
sets.description:
Gallade is an offensive threat thanks to Sharpness and its ability to set Trick Room up for itself and its partners.
Sacred Sword ignores the Defense boosts of foes like Archaludon and Dondozo. Psycho Cut is used as a consistent Psychic-type STAB move that is also boosted by Sharpness. Its damage can be boosted…

sets.name: Trick Room Abuser
sets.description:
Psycho Cut and Sacred Sword are the highest base power moves that take advantage of Sharpness.
The Defense EVs allow Gallade to survive a Wicked Blow and Surging Strikes from Urshifu-S and Urshifu-R, respectively.
Tera Grass allows Gallade to ignore powder moves from Amoonguss, a common Trick Room counter, and resist…

sets.name: All-out Attacker
sets.description:
With its wide range of moveset options and its offense-boosting ability Sharpness, Gallade is excellent at dueling a wide variety of threats. Especially notable is Scared Sword, letting Gallade ignore Stamina Archaludon's accumulated boosts.
Night Slash versus Knock Off: Night Slash's consistent power is best for pure…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description:
With Choice Scarf, Gallade is a decent anti-lead against several early-game setups and an unexpected revenge killer.
Knock Off versus Night Slash: Knock Off remains a strong disruption move with or without Trick, provided you exercise caution if you've already used Trick. Night Slash has more offensive presence than…

478.name: Froslass
478.overview: Froslass's access to Spikes alongside a plethora of utility options makes it an excellent setter on both offensive and balance teams. It excels as a dedicated lead on hyper offense teams, providing early entry hazards while generating momentum through Destiny Bond. Alternatively, its bulkier sets can check physical…

sets.name: Spikes Lead
sets.description: Froslass's rare combination of high Speed, vast utility movepool, and its typing, which lets it take advantage of spinners like Tatsugiri and Alolan Sandslash and aids it in beating the Defoggers such as Scyther and Altaria one-on-one, helps it solidify a niche as a dedicated hyper offense lead that can beat common…

sets.name: Lead
sets.description: Froslass functions as a dedicated offensive lead thanks to its Ghost typing, which blocks Rapid Spin, and its wide array of utility options, allowing it to reliably set Spikes to wear down opposing teams and make wallbreakers such as Lycanroc, Toxicroak, and Jolteon more difficult to switch into. Taunt prevents entry…

479.name: Rotom-Heat
479.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out this page!
If you feel this analysis is missing anything important or needs an update, feel free to suggest any changes here.

sets.name: Choice Attacker
sets.description:
Rotom-H sets itself apart from other Fire-types, with its Ground immunity and Volt Switch pivoting. When compared to Rotom-W, it is also much more effective at pressuring Flutter Mane, Gholdengo and Wo-Chien.
Trick: Trick gives Rotom-H an excellent tool against defensive foes, such as Dondozo and Skeledirge.
Using a…

sets.name: Defensive Pivot
sets.description:
Rotom-W's defensive set might be the more known one, but Rotom-H's key resistances to Fairy-type attacks and U-turn, plus its ability to put serious pressure on Gholdengo, make it a good alternative as a bulky pivot.
Foul Play: Foul Play is a must-have, as otherwise, Rotom-H is extremely passive against Baxcalibur,…

sets.name: Offensive
sets.description:
Rotom-W's Speed access to Volt Switch and great typing make it an excellent pivot and revenge killer.
Trick and Neutral Natures: If using a Choice item, Trick is seriously worth considering for its ability to cripple defensive Pokemon such as Garganacl and Dondozo. Make sure Rotom-W is a neutral nature that has had a…

sets.name: Defensive Pivot
sets.description:
Levitate and excellent status moves make Rotom-W a clean choice for a bulky pivot!
Foul Play: Foul Play is essential for hitting Baxcalibur hard, since it's immunity to burns would otherwise let it set up on Rotom-W.
Will-O-Wisp versus Thunder Wave: Will-O-Wisp's ability to cripple top-tier physically offensive…

480.name: Uxie
480.overview: Uxie's deep movepool and solid bulk carve a role out for it as either a bulky setup sweeper or utility pivot. Nasty Plot sets have an easy time setting up thanks to Uxie's high defensive stats, which also make them good candidates for defensive Terastallization. Options like Encore, U-turn, Stealth Rock, and Knock Off…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description:
Set Details
Psychic Noise helps Uxie suffocate walls like Sylveon, Tera Steel Wo-Chien, and Vaporeon, somewhat compensating for its passivity. Draining Kiss covers Dark-types, but it's primarily used in tandem with Terastallization to increase Uxie's longevity. Encore can shut down walls and utility Pokemon like…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description:
Set Details
Uxie is a decent bulky pivot capable of compressing plenty of utility onto one set. As Uxie isn't an especially strong attacker, Psychic Noise is helpful to smother bulkier Pokemon with recovery moves, such as Vaporeon and Bellibolt. Knock Off punishes Pokemon that depend on their items, such as Gligar,…

481.name: Mesprit
481.overview: Mesprit is a highly flexible Pokemon that is capable of fulfulling a multitude of roles within the ZU metagame. Its balanced stats and useful movepool allow Mesprit to work well in offensive and defensive utility roles, while its Nasty Plot set is a threatening sweeper that is extremely challenging for bulky builds to…

sets.name: Utility
sets.description:
Mesprit can opt not to hold an item to reduce the damage it takes from Knock Off and, thanks to Levitate, wall Golurk. However, Mesprit generally prefers having an item to increase its overall reliability.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description:
Running a third coverage option expands the range of foes Mesprit can break through. For example, Shadow Ball can help it get past opposing Psychic-types in Galarian Slowbro and Hoopa, while Grass Knot OHKOes Rhydon and Mudsdale and smacks Milotic.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

sets.name: Physically Defensive Pivot
sets.description: Mesprit acts as a great defensive pivot thanks to its solid physical bulk with investment, as it can check physical attackers like Hisuian Sneasel and Primeape. On top of Mesprit's bulk and defensive profile enabling it to act as a reliable Stealth Rock setter, it also has good matchups against common entry hazard…

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Choice Scarf Mesprit is a great revenge killer to handle fast Pokemon like Jolteon and Hisuian Sneasel. Dazzling Gleam is Mesprit's best complementary coverage move for Psychic, covering Dark-types like Sneasel and Sableye. U-turn lets Mesprit keep up offensive momentum against switch-ins like Orthworm, Farigiraf, and…

sets.name: Choice Specs
sets.description: Dialga's unique typing and good offensive stats give it a niche as a strong wallbreaker that is also able to soft check Pokemon such as Primal Kyogre, Eternatus, Palkia-O, and utility Arceus formes. However, its middling Speed and weakness to common Ground-types such as Primal Groudon and Arceus-Ground mean Dialga can…

sets.name: Wallbreaker
sets.description: Palkia-O uses its powerful Water / Dragon STAB combination, further bolstered by Lustrous Globe, its high Special Attack, supplemented with Fire-type coverage, to become a nuclear wallbreaker that excels at eviscerating the defensive backbone of many balance teams. Palkia-O is capable of 2HKOing defensive stalwarts…

485.name: Heatran
485.overview:
Heatran is a niche bulky offensive threat thanks to its solid stats and great typing, bolstered by Flash Fire. This makes Heatran have an incredible matchup against most of the metagame, such as Flutter Mane and Gholdengo.
Rillaboom is a great partner for Heatran, as it resists Heatran's prominent weaknesses, while…

sets.name: Bulky Attacker
sets.description: The combination of Heatran's STAB moves, Heat Wave and Flash Cannon, can hit a lot of Pokemon in the metagame super-effectively, while Earth Power is a good coverage move that can hit Pokemon that resist Heatran's STAB combination, like Incineroar. Magma Storm is also another option as a Fire-type attack, as it traps…

487.name: Giratina-Origin
487.overview: Giratina-O is most notable for being the only offensive Defog user in Ubers, giving it a distinct niche. Its high bulk and unique typing combined with Levitate give it great use against top metagame threats like Arceus-Ground, Extreme Killer Arceus, and Kyogre. While its offensive stats are a bit low by Ubers…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Thanks to its bulk, typing, and Levitate ability, defensive Giratina-O checks Pokemon like Arceus, Arceus-Ground, and Choice Scarf Kyogre, and it is the only metagame-relevant Defog user. Will-O-Wisp and Thunder Wave both debilitate physical attackers, but Will-O-Wisp chips away at walls and affects Dragon Dance…

sets.name: Physical Utility
sets.description: Although it necessitates teammates like Necrozma-DM to not run Knock Off, Poltergeist is a strong Ghost-type attack that is very spammable due to how few Pokemon that resist Ghost there are in the tier. Dragon Tail is a decently strong Dragon-type attack that has utility for forcing out Extreme Killer Arceus, which…

488.name: Cresselia
488.overview:
Cresselia shines in VGC as an incredibly bulky Trick Room setter with access to Lunar Blessing. Cresselia is a great check to Amoonguss and Urshifu-R, nullifying Spore with Lunar Blessing and pressuring the latter with a 2HKO.
Cresselia's strongest partner is Ursaluna, as it's a strong Trick Room sweeper that…

sets.name: Trick Room Support
sets.description:
Cresselia uses Moonblast to hit more of the metagame for super effective damage, including Urshifu-S, while Ice Beam can be used to threaten the Landorus formes and Dragonite. Cresselia may opt for Psychic for the extra power before Terastallization.
Helping Hand allows Cresselia to more actively support its partners,…

sets.name: Trick Room
sets.description: This EV spread survives a Dark Pulse from Timid Choice Specs Chi-Yu.

sets.name: Serene Queen (Calm Mind)
sets.description:
With its great bulk and the advent of Terastallization, Cresselia is a formidable defensive presence with the ability to take over late in the game.
Calm Mind: Calm Mind allows Cresselia to bolster its defensive profile while also increasing its damage output.
Moonblast: While lacking STAB, Moonblast has no…

sets.name: Tail Glow
sets.description: Tail Glow Manaphy puts the spotlight on the power of Tail Glow combined with Manaphy's deep movepool, transforming it into a powerful wallbreaker. With a single Tail Glow, Manaphy's Surf washes away common walls, 2HKOing Ting-Lu, Galarian Slowking, and Assault Vest Iron Crown, as well as threatening to OHKO offensive…

sets.name: Take Heart
sets.description: Take Heart Manaphy takes advantage of Manaphy's decent bulk and strong arsenal of boosting moves to power its way through weakened teams. Take Heart and Acid Armor form a potent combination that allows Manaphy to quickly become unassailable and snowball through unprepared teams, especially with Take Heart clearing…

sets.name: Offensive Setup Sweeper

Outspeeding the entirety of OU's metagame, it can threaten Deoxys-S and Dragapult as well as boosted threats like Dragonite, Iron Valiant, and Iron Moth. Sludge…

sets.description: Darkrai's survivability due to its immunity to entry hazards alongside the combination of an excellent Speed tier, strong offensive pressure, and access to a bevy of utility options allow it to function quite effectively as a potent special attacker on longer games. sets.name: Choice Specs

493.name: Arceus
493.overview: Arceus is one of the best setup sweepers and revenge killers in AG thanks to its STAB-boosted Extreme Speed. Extreme Speed allows Arceus to act as a revenge killer, making it problematic for opposing setup sweepers like Miraidon and Scale Shot Koraidon. Thanks to its Normal typing and bulk, it can reliably take on…

Arceus has arrived to take its rightful place as the undisputed king of Battle Stadium Singles, unleashing the true power of Extreme Speed in ways Dragonite could only dream of.

sets.name: Bulky Swords Dance

sets.name: Choice Scarf
sets.description: Choice Scarf Victini functions as a niche late-game cleaner and revenge killer, due to its ability to outspeed Pokemon such as Tapu Koko and Mega Lopunny as well as threaten important defensive pieces such as Ferrothorn and Gholdengo with V-create. U-turn is used to gain momentum on switch-ins such as Heatran,…

sets.name: Pivot

sets.description: Z-Celebrate Victini acts as a niche setup sweeper, where its combination of Z-Celebrate and Stored Power can make it a threatening late-game cleaner. Z-Celebrate's omniboost makes Victini a potent sweeper due to its enhanced bulk and offenses making it difficult to revenge kill. When combined with Z-Celebrate, Stored…

sets.name: Pivot
sets.description: Heavy-Duty Boots Victini is a great offensive pivot, using the combination of its strong V-create, utility options, and momentum to wear down opposing teams. Bolt Strike provides crucial coverage against Water-types such as Slowbro and Volcanion that may try to soak up its other moves. It also OHKOes Mega Aerodactyl,…

497.name: Serperior
497.overview:
Welcome to Battle Stadium Singles Regulation F! This Pokemon is a new addition from the Indigo Disk that happens to be relevant!
If you feel that this analysis is missing important information, feel free to leave feedback here.

With STAB Leaf Storm paired with its incredible ability in Contrary, Serperior is a formidable wallbreaker and potential sweeper.
Leaf Storm: Thanks to Contrary, Leaf Storm will boost Serperior’s Special Attack by two stages, rather than lowering it, allowing Serperior to become more of a threat simply by attacking.…

500.name: Emboar

Fighting
Emboar is unique on Fighting teams in its ability to be a good Fairy-neutral special attack sponge that is immune to getting burned. Compared to fellow Assault Vest users like Iron Hands and Gallade, however, it lacks a useful secondary offensive typing that allows it to break through common…

sets.description: Emboar makes use of Choice Scarf to counteract its middling Speed stat, becoming a terrifying revenge killer. Fire Blast lets Emboar power through a would-be check in Skarmory and also hits Forretress without suffering from recoil damage or Rocky…

503.overview: Hisuian Samurott is one of the most prolific entry hazard setters in the tier with its signature move Ceaseless Edge, letting it both set Spikes and deal heavy damage thanks to its Sharpness ability. It can even do so in the face of Hatterene's Magic Bounce and Taunt from Pokemon like Deoxys-S, something that…

Though weaker, Aqua Cutter is a perfectly-accurate alternative over Razor Shell that notably ignores common contact effects like Landorus-T's Rocky Helmet, Heatran's Flame Body, and Zapdos's Static at the cost of potentially…

Razor Shell and Aqua Cutter are comparably strong STAB options, the difference being Razor Shell trades a slight dip of accuracy for the ability to chip down walls with Defense…

Razor Shell can make Hisuian Samurott more difficult to respond to due to its high chance to lower the enemy's Defense. However, Aqua Cutter can be used instead, as it is more accurate and does not make…

Iron Treads provides more utility, as well as being able to OHKO Landorus with Ice Spinner, while Excadrill provides more immediate power and has fewer switch-ins thanks to Mold…

Rock Slide complements Earthquake by hitting checks like Zapdos and Moltres super effectively while granting a neutral coverage against Ogerpon-W and Corviknight.…

Iron Head is preferred over Rock Slide to let Excadrill break past Hydrapple and Sinistcha, while Rock Slide is useful for Mandibuzz and Skarmory. Rapid Spin is used both…

After a Swords Dance, Iron Head does heavy damage to Hydrapple, Mandibuzz, and Tornadus-T. Rapid Spin is primarily used for…

547.overview: Blessed with the rare Fairy typing and an excellent Speed tier, Whimsicott makes for an excellent revenge killer and utility Pokemon. Whimsicott's unique typing allows it to beat many dangerous threats like Persian-Alola and Virizion. A powerful Moonblast and pivoting coupled with Choice Specs allow Whimsicott to…

Whimsicott is a staple on hyper offense teams due to its ability to set Tailwind with its Prankster ability. Whimsicott also provides valuable support to its team through the use of Encore to disrupt opposing strategies or Helping Hand and Sunny Day to boost its partner's damage output.

Fighting

Flare Blitz provides coverage against Steel-types including Corviknight and…

560.name: Scrafty
560.overview: Thanks to its solid mixed bulk, many resistances, and great abilities, Scrafty is a potent setup sweeper. Both Bulk Up and Dragon Dance sets are major setup threats because of Scrafty's access to STAB Knock Off, Close Combat, and Drain Punch for great overall power, utility, and sustainability. This Gothitelle set aims to trap and PP stall weak walls so that its offensive teammates can take over mid- to late-game. This Gothitelle set can come in on weak walls like Clodsire, Ting-Lu, Alomomola, and defensive Great Tusk and beat them by PP stalling them.
Charm and Calm Mind are use to boost Gothitelle's…

sets.name: Trapper

It lets Gothitelle trap common walls, such as defensive Ho-Oh and Coil Zygarde, forcing them to use Struggle, and it can deny setup from certain sweepers such as Ultra Necrozma. The ability for it to trap many key…

579.name: Reuniclus
579.overview: Reuniclus is a versatile Pokemon that can either serve as a potentially devastating late-game wincon with its offensive sets or as a defensive tank that can utilize its naturally good bulk and a multitude of utility options to support its teammates. Both of its abilities are fantastic; Magic Guard's immunity to…

Psychic Noise denies recovery from bulky Pokemon like Gliscor, Garganacl, and Dondozo as well as bypassing Substitute against attackers like Kyurem and Iron Moth. Knock Off…

sets.description: Calm Mind Reuniclus is a potent late-game cleaner that is difficult to break past for weakened teams. Reuniclus is unable to be dealt with through forms of passive damage such as Spikes, Toxic, and Leech Seed, which many defensive teams rely on to wear down opposing threats. While slow, Reuniclus sports good bulk when…

sets.description: Defensive Reuniclus finds a place in a metagame lacking phenomenal Dark-types outside of Krookodile and Bisharp and with a myriad of Fighting- and Psychic- types such as Terrakion, Mienshao, Slowbro and Jirachi. Its bulk and typing allow it to continuously switch into the aforementioned Fighting- and Psychic-types'…

590.name: Foongus
590.overview: Due to its good defensive typing, excellent ability, and utility provided by Spore, Foongus is one of the best defensive Pokemon in Little Cup. With the longevity provided by Regenerator and Synthesis, Foongus is able to reliably check many threats over the course of the game, including Chinchou, Grookey, and Mienfoo.…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Giga Drain and Sludge Bomb make for an effective STAB combination capable of hitting most of the tier for at least neutral damage. Sludge Bomb's chance for poison can ruin potential switch-ins such as Mienfoo or Toedscool. Synthesis provides extra longevity so Foongus doesn't solely rely on Regenerator, as Giga Drain…

591.name: Amoonguss
591.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out this page!
If you feel this analysis is missing anything important or needs an update, feel free to suggest any changes here.

sets.name: Bulky Support
sets.description:
Amoonguss's main purpose is to disrupt the opposing team with Spore while protecting teammates with Rage Powder and healing them through Pollen Puff.
Protect is the best fourth move most of the time because it means that the opponent can not freely double target Amoonguss. Clear Smog can be used as an answer to…

sets.name: Bulky Support
sets.description: Amoonguss's great typing, access to Regenerator, and excellent toolkit allow it to function as a top-notch support Pokemon with the ability to check and, with Spore, shut down Raging Bolt, Urshifu-R, and Zamazenta-C throughout the game. Amoonguss uses Pollen Puff to heal its teammates, providing its team the upper…

sets.description: Amoonguss is the tier’s premier redirector, neutralizing foes with Spore and sustaining its teammates with Pollen Puff. Its defensive prowess, coupled with Regenerator, provides the sustainability to soak up hits while supporting its team. Amoonguss pairs incredibly well with setup attackers such as Bulk Up Palafin-H…

sets.description:
The given EV spread allows Amoonguss to survive a Heat Wave from Mega Charizard Y, allocating the remaining EVs into Defense.
An alternative EV spread of 240 HP / 208 Def / 60 SpD allows Amoonguss to survive an Icicle Crash from Chien-Pao.

sets.description:
With Wish and a slow Flip Turn, Alomomola has a strong combination for providing team healing.
Protect: Alomomola can use Wish and Protect to give itself reliable self-recovery, and this combination makes Alomomola great at stalling out turns against weather, Terrains, and Trick Room.

Flip Turn and Regenerator combine to make Alomomola one of the hardest pivots to efficiently take out.
Mirror Coat: Alomomola makes excellent use of Mirror Coat with its high base HP, retaliating comfortably against top special attackers like Ursaluna-B and Raging Bolt.
Scald: Scald's moderate burn chance makes…

sets.description: Alomomola's gargantuan HP stat, good defensive typing, a slow Flip Turn, and Regenerator make it one of the best defensive pivots available that can reliably pass Wishes to its teammates. On top of reliably gaining recovery from Wish, Protect has many more applications for Alomomola; it allows Alomomola to scout…

sets.description: Alomomola can use Assault Vest to improve its poor Special Defense, becoming a specially defensive pivot at the expense of Wish. Alomomola can soak up most moves from special attackers like Dragapult, Darkrai, and Iron Moth and pivot out with Flip Turn to the appropriate teammate, particularly frail offensive ones,…

Thunder, combined with the accuracy boost from Compound Eyes, is Galvantula's strongest move, which hits Water-types such as Toxapex and deters Flying-type…

Thunder, combined with the accuracy boost from Compound Eyes, is Galvantula's strongest move, which hits Water-types such as Toxapex and deters Flying-type Defoggers such as Corviknight.…

sets.description: Galvantula serves as an excellent Sticky Web lead, supporting its offensive team members with speed control. Thunder in conjunction with Compound Eyes gives Galvantula an offensive presence, takes on opposing hazard-setting leads, threatens paralysis, and dents Water- and Flying-types. Energy Ball threatens bulky…

sets.name: Defensive
sets.description: Ferrothorn is an excellent defensive Steel-type and entry hazard setter, since its Grass typing lends it resistances to Water and Electric as well as a neutrality to Ground, allowing it to more comfortably handle Pokemon like Ogerpon-W, Mega Swampert, Tapu Koko, and Raging Bolt, which other Steel-types like Mega…

Ferrothorn serves as a sturdy roadblock to wallbreaker Primal Kyogre, which otherwise easily overwhelms most…

It's an incredible asset for Grass teams, as it can manage notable threats such as Kyurem, Tapu Lele, and Melmetal. Gyro Ball capitalizes on its low Speed and threatens Fairy-types and fast foes…

Protect allows Ferrothorn to maximize the HP it gains per turn between Leftovers and Leech Seed, as well as easing prediction against Choice-locked foes such…

Chandelure, donning a Choice Specs, easily breaks through would-be checks like Rhyperior and Goodra. However, with Choice Scarf, it becomes a dangerous revenge killer that’s able to…

Icicle Spear's higher power is preferred over Icicle Crash's potential flinch chance if running Loaded Dice, and its…

sets.name: Fast Pivot

Knock Off enables Mienfoo to create consistent progress against opposing Pokemon like Foongus and Mareanie that resist its Fighting-type attacks.…

Fake Out provides useful chip damage, while High Jump Kick is its STAB of choice, pressuring most of the metagame. The EV…

sets.description: Swords Dance Mienfoo is one of the tier's scariest wallbreakers and sweepers. +2 High Jump Kick will OHKO almost all targets that do not resist it, with the exception of fully healthy Mudbray and Vullaby with at least 156 Defense EVs. Knock Off provides nearly perfect coverage against Ghost-types and Terastallized…

sets.description: Choice Scarf Mienfoo's superb Speed tier, in conjunction with its Reckless-boosted High Jump Kick, makes it an excellent wallbreaker capable of OHKOing or 2HKOing most neutral targets. sets.name: Offensive Utility
sets.description: Mienshao is a fast Pokemon with a good support movepool that matches up well into Incineroar, since it is immune to Fake Out and Intimidate. sets.name: Life Orb
sets.description: Mienshao functions as an elite offensive pivot that can fire off its high-powered moves while being hard to chip down thanks to Regenerator offsetting Life Orb recoil. It brings value in threatening key Pokemon such as Rotom-W, Skarmory, and Hippowdon. Knock Off in conjunction with U-turn wears down checks such as…

sets.description: Choice Scarf Mienshao trades some power for speed, expanding its revenge killing capabilities because it is now able to threaten much faster Pokemon like Mega Aerodactyl, Serperior, and Zeraora. Knock Off punishes switch-ins like Slowbro and Aegislash, leaving them more vulnerable to entry hazards from teammates like…

623.name: Golurk
623.overview: An incredible Attack stat, offensive typing, and unique coverage options give Golurk a niche as a strong wallbreaker that can dismantle common defensive staples, such as Slowbro, Gastrodon, and Hisuian Goodra. Golurk bears the coveted combination of Dynamic Punch and No Guard, deterring Dark-types like Bisharp and…

sets.name: Wallbreaker
sets.description:
Set Details
Golurk is a niche but effective wallbreaker in the tier, using its powerful STAB moves and coverage options to blow holes through defensive cores with ease. Dynamic Punch, with the accuracy assurance granted by No Guard, allows Golurk to disrupt foes that otherwise wall it and hits Dark-types like Umbreon…

sets.name: Wallbreaker
sets.description:
Set Details
Both Dynamic Punch and Close Combat are used to target the main stopgaps to Golurk's powerful STAB attacks, namely Wo-Chien, Brute Bonnet, and Dudunsparce. Dynamic Punch capitalizes on Golurk's No Guard to confuse foes, helping against offensive switch-ins like Paldean Tauros-W and Flygon, whereas Close…

sets.name: Choice Band
sets.description: Golurk is a potent wallbreaker with limited switch-ins thanks to its incredible STAB combination, which is resisted by only a few Pokemon in the tier. Golurk excels at dismantling common defensive cores thanks to its high Attack and great typing, which let it switch into and force out defensive staples like Galarian…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description: Pawniard is a solid defensive Pokemon with the potential to win games. Swords Dance can be used against Pokemon such as on Vullaby, Gastly, and Snubbull. Sucker Punch is a strong STAB priority move that allows Pawniard to hit foes that would otherwise outspeed it such as Gastly and Snivy. Tera Flying lets Pawniard…

sets.description: Pawniard is a solid utility Pokemon, pairing its excellent typing that allows it to check common Pokemon like Vullaby with its useful support options in Stealth Rock and Thunder Wave. Thunder Wave keeps Pawniard from being too passive, and it combines well with Iron Head to paraflinch opposing Pokemon. Iron Head lets…

625.name: Bisharp
625.overview: Bisharp stands out as one of the premier setup sweepers in the metagame due its access to Swords Dance, strong priority in Sucker Punch, and impressive Eviolite-boosted bulk. Coupled with a fantastic defensive typing, Bisharp's bulk allows it to tank attacks from some of the most common Pokemon in the tier, including…

sets.name: Swords Dance
sets.description:
Set Details
Swords Dance allows Bisharp to serve as a fantastic late-game sweeper. Sucker Punch provides strong priority that enables Bisharp to pick off weakened sweepers like Gengar and improves its ability to clean. Eviolite drastically boosts Bisharp's ability to take hits, which can be invaluable for setting up.…

sets.description: When paired with an Eviolite, Bisharp is capable of taking on the role of a bulky setup sweeper that also discourages the use of Defog. Additonally, Bisharp's Dark typing allows Eviolite to ease the matchup against Ghost teams by letting Bisharp take on their Ghost-type STAB moves better with its improved bulk. Throat…

sets.name: Eviolite Swords Dance (Dark)
sets.description: Bisharp's extra bulk provided by Eviolite allows it to lure in unsuspecting checks to the non-Eviolite Bisharp set such as Ogerpon-H and Choice Specs Flutter Mane and set up on them with Swords Dance or KO them outright. Furthermore, its Steel typing is incredibly valuable, as it enables it to take Fairy-type attacks…

sets.name: Swords Dance (Dark)
sets.description: Bisharp serves a niche role on Dark teams with its secondary Steel typing and Defiant ability enabling it to take on the bulky Calm Mind Fairy-types of the tier such as Primarina and Clefable and punish Defog from the likes of Mandibuzz or Sticky Web from Araquanid or Galvantula, respectively. At +2 Attack, Iron Head…

sets.name: Bulk Up
sets.description: Braviary outpaces offensive Sylveon and Incineroar with its 96 Speed EVs. Tera Electric is a great defensive Tera type, but it doesn't offer as many useful resistances as Tera Ground and Steel do. Defog sets can be run, but there are better forms of removal that aren't as easily taken advantage of by Galarian Articuno…

sets.description:
Heat Wave OHKOes Alolan Sandslash, which can be useful for opening up a teammate like Florges. Vacuum Wave still deals heavy damage to Alolan Sandslash and also picks off weakened foes like Zoroark and Ambipom.
Sample set. Stay tuned for updates!

sets.name: Physically Defensive (Normal)
sets.description: Braviary serves as good entry hazard removal for Normal teams due to its good bulk and Fighting neutrality, which let it comfortably use Defog in front of foes like Sneasler and Meowscarada. Brave Bird is Braviary's strongest STAB move, threatening to OHKO foes like the Ogerpon formes and Sinistcha. U-turn helps keep…

629.name: Vullaby
629.overview: Vullaby is Little Cup's glue gal: splashable and flexible. It is a switch-in to Ground-types, Knock Off absorber, wallbreaker, pivot, and sweeper all bundled into one skull diaper. Vullaby is the main offensive check to most of LC's physical threats because it can punish attacks it survives with Weak Armor. At +2…

sets.description: Roost allows Vullaby to heal on switches for solid longevity. It can also leverage its natural bulk to recover HP against foes threatening super effective attacks by shedding its Flying typing, which is particularly useful against Volt Switch users after a Weak Armor boost. Using Endure earns Vullaby the chance to KO…

sets.name: Nasty Plot
sets.description: With Air Slash and a coverage move of its choice, a +2 Special Attack Vullaby can OHKO metagame staples like Foongus after Stealth Rock chip damage and try and flinch through other threats with Air Slash. Heat Wave KOes Alolan Sandshrew, does massive damage to bulky variants of Eviolite Tinkatink, and covers for Tera…

sets.name: Defensive

sets.name: Specially Defensive
sets.description: Mandibuzz is one of stall teams' best Calyrex-S checks, utilizing its Dark typing, special bulk, and access to reliable recovery in Roost to wall all Calyrex-S sets, bar Tera Fairy. Toxic significantly cripples foes by limiting how much they can set up. Whirlwind phazes setup threats like various setup Arceus formes.…

Foul Play lets it threaten out Pokemon like Metagross, Excadrill, Scizor, and Belly…

sets.name: Physically Defensive (Dark)
sets.description: Mandibuzz is a decent supportive piece for Dark teams with its ability to pivot into physical attackers like Ceruledge, Garchomp, and Excadrill due to its great bulk and U-turn to position its other teammates into favorable matchups. Foul Play punishes strong physical attackers like Ogerpon-W, Dragonite, and Scizor.…

635.name: Hydreigon
635.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out the format page!

sets.name: Glass Cannon
sets.description:
Hydreigon uses its high Special Attack stat and good Speed stat to make it one of the faster and stronger threats in the current metagame. With its good offensive typing, Hydreigon ends up being a large threat to much of the metagame, such as Gholdengo and Garchomp.

Hydreigon's useful support movepool can add quite a bit of unpredictability to its offensive potential.
Note that this Hydreigon is fully capable of running three attacking moves with one support move.

sets.description: Volcarona acts as a dangerous setup sweeper through Quiver Dance, allowing it to sweep games. Volt Switch allows Cobalion to pivot out on would-be switch-ins such as Latios…

640.overview: Virizion's great Speed tier and threatening offensive typing make it a dangerous offensive threat that can serve as both a wallbreaker and a late-game cleaner. Virizion has access to Swords Dance, which allows it to boost its attack and wallbreak consistently. Its great Speed tier lets it stay threatening versus…

Zen Headbutt can be used instead to threaten Poison-types like Weezing and Muk. Justified dissuades foes such as Primeape and Poliwrath from attempting to remove Virizion's item…

641.overview: Tornadus's high-Base Power STAB and coverage moves, which are both further bolstered by its gargantuan offenses, and access to amazing utility options like Knock Off and Taunt make it a versatile threat in NU. Its Speed is also enormous, letting it blow past common fast Pokemon like Alolan Ninetales, Munkidori, and…

Eerie Impulse can slow down powerful special attackers more effectively than other forms of damage control like Snarl or Vessel of Ruin, which means that Thundurus can completely neutralize the likes of Calyrex-S,…

Grass Knot: Grass Knot is highly recommended for dealing massive damage to Ground-types, such as Ting-Lu and Ursaluna, without Terastallizing in addition to being a secondary attack to hit Urshifu-R while…

These make it a great Choice Band sweeper, threatening many common metagame threats such as Raging Bolt and Flutter Mane. Landorus-T can also function as a Intimidate pivoter, using its great…

Set Details

Thanks to Meloetta's range, it can be unpredictable at team preview and early-game. It's also an excellent user of…

However, as direct competition to Bug's other special attacking options, even ignoring Volcarona, Vivillon…

Hurricane deals with annoying Fighting- and Bug-types such as Iron Valiant and Lokix as long as its Focus Sash is still intact. Sleep Powder…

678.name: Meowstic
678.overview:
=========
Outmatched by Deoxys-S, Meowstic-M is an inferior screens setter option for Psychic. Due to its lack of utility options like Knock Off, Taunt, Stealth Rock, and Spikes, Meowstic provides little outside of dual screens and Thunder Wave. However, with its ability Prankster, Meowstic can reliably set up dual…

sets.name: Dual Screens Setter
sets.description: Boasting useful utility in dual screens and a complementary ability in Prankster, Meowstic-M is a niche dual screens setter for Psychic teams. Light Screen and Reflect mitigate damage for teammates attempting to set up, prolonging survivability and amplifying offensive capability. Charm helps deal with physical…

sets.name: SubToxic

Substitute allows Aegislash to take advantage of the aforementioned Pokemon's inability to deal sufficient damage to it…

sets.name: Offensive Swords Dance

Close Combat is Aegislash's strongest move overall, and it threatens the many Dark-types that would otherwise resist its Ghost-type STAB…

sets.name: Mixed Attacker
sets.description: Aegislash's excellent offenses and deep coverage allow it to function as an effective mixed attacker, slicing through a huge portion of the metagame, including defensive staples such as Mega Latias, Slowbro, and Blissey. Additionally, Aegislash's superb defensive traits mean it takes relatively little from their…

sets.name: Bulky Swords Dance
sets.description: Bulky Swords Dance Aegislash serves as a terrifying approach to its traditional role as a wallbreaker and setup sweeper, giving up immediate threat potential for incredible resilience. Sylveon's solid natural bulk as well as access to absurd damage from Pixilate-boosted Hyper Voice makes it a solid setup sweeper. With its pure Fairy typing, Sylveon boasts a better matchup into Ghost- and Dark-type threats such as Flutter Mane and Urshifu-S than its main Fairy-type competition, Flutter Mane.

Set Details

Zygarde’s unparalleled bulk enables it to counter some of the scariest…

Volcanion is a solid option for Water teams thanks to its unique typing and outstanding offenses. What it lacks in utility, it makes up for in sheer power if given the right opportunities to wallbreak, easily dismantling common defensive cores on Steel and Flying. However, Water has no shortage of great special…

Flip Turn: Flip Turn is perfect for letting Palafin do some chip damage and…

sets.name: Bulk Up
sets.description:
Palafin-H's powerful priority and good Speed tier separate it from other Bulk Up sweepers, such as Annihilape.
Drain Punch: Even without Tera Fighting, Drain Punch's extra healing makes it a great choice for coverage, and it does massive damage to Kingambit, Chien-Pao, and Meowscarada.

Revavroom's typing helps it find setup opportunities against Pokemon like Ogerpon-C and Scizor, and it carries helpful resistances against the priority moves of revenge killers like Lokix, Weavile, and Scizor. 967.name: Cyclizar
967.overview: Cyclizar rides in as one of the defining Pokemon of the RU metagame. Its excellent Speed tier, access to Rapid Spin, and ability Regenerator make it the premier entry hazard removal option. It can also break out its utility toolbox, threatening to remove items from common switch-ins like Jirachi and Slowbro with Knock…

sets.name: Assault Vest
sets.description: Assault Vest Cyclizar can safely switch into most special attackers and provide opportunities for its teammates to enter the game thanks to U-turn. Rapid Spin clears entry hazards while also granting Cyclizar a boost to Speed, which helps it outspeed most Choice Scarf users in the tier like Gardevoir and Gengar. It…

sets.name: Heavy-Duty Boots
sets.description: While usually overshadowed by the more prevalent Assault Vest set, running Heavy-Duty Boots on Cyclizar can offer meaningful benefits, allowing access to utility moves, while also making Regenerator more beneficial without needing to worry about the passive chip that comes with its usage as a spinner. 968.name: Orthworm
968.overview: Orthworm's place in ZU is defined by its extremely valuable Steel typing combined with its amazing natural Defense and an immunity to Ground-type moves through its signature ability, Earth Eater. Orthworm faciliates setup sweepers on hyper offense teams thanks to its Ground immunity and access to Shed Tail. Unlike Cyclizar, Orthworm is able to pass slow Shed Tails due to its low Speed.
Orthworm has access to both Stealth Rock and Spikes, helping it wear down walls for its setup sweeper teammates. Stealth Rock…

969.name: Glimmet
969.overview: Glimmet's unique ability, Toxic Debris, coupled with its access to both Stealth Rock and Spikes, cements it as one of Little Cup's most reliable entry hazard setters. Glimmet also packs the highest Special Attack in the tier, making for an excellent progress-making piece on offensive teams. Its defensive typing and…

sets.name: Hazard Setter
sets.description: Power Gem OHKOes Vullaby after Stealth Rock chip damage, while Spikes punishes opposing teams without entry hazard removal. Mud Shot provides coverage for opposing Steel- and Poison-type Pokemon and slows down faster foes or switch-ins, granting Glimmet more chances to move. Toxic Debris sets Toxic Spikes when Glimmet…

970.name: Glimmora
970.overview: Glimmora is a strong offensive threat with access to a good STAB combination and the powerful move Meteor Beam, which, when used with the Power Herb, can melt many targets, such as Assault Vest Entei and Incineroar. sets.name: Meteor Beam
sets.description:

sets.name: Bulky Attacker
sets.description:
Both Earth Power and Energy Ball are fine options, but Energy Ball can do more damage against neutral targets if Glimmora Terastallizes into a Grass type.
Mortal Spin is used as the final move for a more consistent method to spread poison than Toxic Debris.

sets.description:
Glimmora's ability, Toxic Debris, discourages physical attackers from attacking it with physical moves. Setting up Toxic Spikes is invaluable against bulky teams, crippling common threats like Ting-Lu, Iron Hands, and Diancie.

972.name: Houndstone
972.overview: Houndstone establishes itself in NU as an incredibly reliable spinblocker and defensive presence, being able to prevent Rapid Spin from the likes of Tsareena while taking on dangerous attackers such as Flamigo and Scyther effortlessly. Houndstone is able to utilize its switch-in opportunities to generate progress via…

973.name: Flamigo
973.overview:
Welcome to the next generation of Battle Stadium Singles! In this new and exciting metagame, new ideas are constantly developing, so this analysis will constantly be updated to reflect metagame trends.

sets.name: Glass Cannon

Flamigo both hits hard and is decently fast, doing particularly well into threats such as Incineroar, Gholdengo, and Sneasler thanks to its ability Scrappy and good offensive typing.

977.name: Dondozo
977.overview: This Pokemon has been updated for the return of the Regulation G format and its powerful restricted threats!

sets.name: Commander Sweeper
sets.description:
Combining with Tatsugiri-C allows Dondozo to become a threatening sweeper, being able to pose a significant threat to the majority of teams. Its significant bulk and attacking prowess allow it to rip through unprepared teams and make it hard to remove without consistent answers.

Dondozo matches up well against many physical attackers, including Incineroar, Zamazenta-C, and…

sets.name: Offensive Spinner
sets.description: While Tatsugiri has many good traits, like its Speed, access to Rapid Spin as well as Nasty Plot, and a great ability in Storm Drain, it usually falls short due to its practically non-existent bulk. However, some hyper offense can work around this glaring issue and capitalize on Tatsugiri's great traits with this set.

979.name: Annihilape

983.name: Kingambit
983.overview:
This analysis is for the Regulation C format of Battle Stadium Singles, which lasts until June 30th, 2023. For more information on this format and its current events, check out this page!

Sucker Punch gives Kingambit a priority attack that it can use to get around its low Speed and that threatens Pokemon like the Calyrex…

1020.name: Gouging Fire
1020.overview:
Gouging Fire is a solid offensive support Pokemon with a good defensive typing. Breaking Swipe and Burning Bulwark give it a great matchup against physical attackers such as Rillaboom and Chien-Pao.

1021.name: Raging Bolt
1021.overview: Assault Vest Raging Bolt makes the most of its titanic statline to check a variety of threats in the tier while leveraging disruptive utility moves to dictate the pace of battle for its teammates. 1023.name: Iron Crown
1023.overview: Iron Crown is an excellent choice for offensive teams looking to take advantage of the powerful move Expanding Force thanks to its well-rounded stats and access to Booster Energy. sets.name: Booster Energy
